AAPL_10K_2022Q3_2022-10-28_full.txt.document_context: Apple Inc. filed its 2022 annual 10-K for the fiscal year ended 2022-09-24. The company designs and sells iPhone, Mac, iPad, Wearables/Home/Accessories, and services such as the App Store, AppleCare, cloud services, and subscriptions including Apple Music, Apple TV+, Apple Fitness+, Apple News+, and Apple Arcade.

sections.Item 1B: Apple Inc. reported that there were no unresolved staff comments in Item 1B of its 2022 Form 10-K annual report filed with the SEC on October 28, 2022. This section indicates that the company had no outstanding comments from the Securities and Exchange Commission staff requiring resolution at the time of filing.
sections.Item 2: Apple Inc. states that its headquarters are in Cupertino, California, and that as of September 24, 2022 it owned or leased facilities and land used for corporate functions, research and development, data centers, retail, and other purposes across the United States and internationally. The company says these existing facilities and equipment support all reportable segments, are in good operating condition, and are suitable for conducting its business.
sections.Item 1: Apple Inc. designs, manufactures, and markets a broad ecosystem of hardware and services, including iPhone smartphones, Mac personal computers, iPad tablets, and Wearables, Home and Accessories such as AirPods, Apple TV, Apple Watch, Beats, and HomePod mini. Its services portfolio includes advertising, AppleCare support, cloud services, and digital content platforms like the App Store, Apple Arcade, Apple Fitness+, Apple Music, Apple News+, and Apple TV+, with products and services built around iOS, macOS, iPadOS, tvOS, and watchOS.
sections.Item 4: Apple Inc. states that Item 4, Mine Safety Disclosures, is not applicable in its 2022 Form 10-K filed with the SEC on October 28, 2022. The section contains no mine safety operations, incidents, citations, or regulatory disclosures, reflecting that Apple is not engaged in mining activities subject to Mine Safety and Health Administration reporting.
sections.Item 3: Apple Inc. describes ongoing legal proceedings centered on Epic Games, Inc.’s antitrust and California unfair competition lawsuit over the App Store in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California, where Apple won 9 of 10 counts and its breach-of-contract counterclaims, but the court enjoined certain App Store Review Guidelines provisions and Epic appealed while Apple filed a cross-appeal and obtained a stay. The filing also notes unspecified ordinary-course litigation and claims, some matters settled in Q4 2022 without material impact, and warns that adverse resolutions could materially affect Apple’s financial condition and operating results.
sections.Item 1A: Apple Inc. says its business, reputation, results of operations, financial condition, and stock price are exposed to a wide range of known and unknown risks, and that historical performance may not predict future results; the Item 1A discussion includes forward-looking statements and should be read with MD&A and the consolidated financial statements. The excerpt emphasizes macroeconomic and industry risks, noting that sales outside the U.S. represent a majority of Apple’s net sales and that its global supply chain is large and complex, with most supplier facilities and manufacturing and assembly sites located outside the U.S. Adverse global or regional conditions such as inflation, recession, tariffs, tighter credit, higher interest rates, unemployment, currency fluctuations, and market volatility could reduce consumer confidence and demand for Apple products and services.
sections.Preamble: Apple Inc. filed this Form 10-K annual report for the fiscal year ended September 24, 2022 under Commission File Number 001-36743, identifying itself as a California corporation headquartered at One Apple Park Way, Cupertino, California 95014, with telephone number (408) 996-1010 and IRS Employer Identification No. 94-2404110. The filing lists Apple’s common stock (AAPL) and multiple Nasdaq-listed debt securities, including notes due 2022, 2024, 2025, 2026, 2027, 2029, 2031, and 2042, and states that Apple is a well-known seasoned issuer that has filed all required Exchange Act reports and interactive data files.
sections.Item 5: Apple Inc. states that its common stock trades on The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC under the symbol AAPL and that it had 23,838 shareholders of record as of October 14, 2022. The section highlights Apple’s active share repurchase program, with 160.172 million shares repurchased during the quarter ended September 24, 2022 at average prices of $145.91, $168.29, and $155.59 per share, leaving $60.665 billion of repurchase capacity and $344.3 billion already utilized under a Board-authorized $405 billion program announced April 28, 2022.
sections.Item 9: Apple Inc. reported no changes in accountants and no disagreements with accountants on accounting or financial disclosure in Item 9 of its 2022 Form 10-K filed with the SEC. The section states “None,” indicating no disputes, restatements, or related issues to disclose.
sections.Item 7A: Apple Inc. says it manages foreign exchange and interest rate risk using foreign currency forward and option contracts and interest rate swaps, but these hedges may offset only part of the impact and their gains or losses may not match the timing of the underlying exposures, potentially affecting financial condition and operating results. Apple’s market risk is driven mainly by U.S. interest rates and its investment portfolio and debt; it invests in highly rated, investment-grade securities to preserve capital and liquidity, and a hypothetical 100 basis point parallel shift in rates would have reduced the portfolio’s fair value by $4.0 billion as of September 24, 2022 and $4.1 billion as of September 25, 2021.
sections.Item 8: Item 8 presents Apple Inc.’s consolidated financial statements and notes for fiscal years ended September 24, 2022, September 25, 2021, and September 26, 2020, including the statements of operations, comprehensive income, balance sheets, shareholders’ equity, cash flows, and the report of the independent registered public accounting firm. The excerpt highlights Apple’s 2022 results: total net sales of $394.3 billion, gross margin of $170.8 billion, operating income of $119.4 billion, net income of $99.8 billion, and diluted EPS of $6.11, with products and services as the two main revenue categories.
sections.Item 9B: During the three months ended September 24, 2022, Apple Inc. reported that five Section 16 officers—Katherine L. Adams, Timothy D. Cook, Luca Maestri, Deirdre O’Brien, and Jeffrey Williams—had equity trading plans in place under Rule 10b5-1(c)(1) of the Exchange Act. The filing explains that these plans are written arrangements that preestablish the amounts, prices, and dates or formulas for future purchases or sales of Apple stock, including shares acquired under the company’s employee and director equity plans.
sections.Item 9A: Apple Inc.’s management, including the principal executive officer and principal financial officer, concluded that disclosure controls and procedures were effective as of September 24, 2022 under Exchange Act Rules 13a-15(e) and 15d-15(e), providing reasonable assurance that required SEC disclosures are recorded, processed, summarized, reported, and communicated on time. The section also explains that Apple’s internal control over financial reporting is designed to provide reasonable assurance regarding GAAP-based financial reporting, accurate transaction records, authorized receipts and expenditures, and prevention or timely detection of unauthorized asset use, while emphasizing inherent limitations and that controls cannot prevent or detect all errors or fraud.
sections.Item 7: Apple Inc.’s Item 7 MD&A for fiscal 2022 focuses on year-over-year results versus 2021, noting total net sales rose 8% to $28.5 billion, driven mainly by higher iPhone, Services, and Mac sales, while foreign currency weakness versus the U.S. dollar negatively affected all Products and Services revenue. The section highlights major 2022 product and software launches including MacBook Pro, AirPods, iPhone SE with 5G, Mac Studio, Studio Display, iPad Air, MacBook Air, iPhone 14 lineup, AirPods Pro, Apple Watch Series 8/SE/Ultra, iOS 16, macOS Ventura, iPadOS 16, watchOS 9, and Apple Pay Later, as well as capital return actions: an increase in share repurchase authorization from $315 billion to $405 billion, a quarterly dividend increase to $0.23 per share, $90.2 billion
sections.Item 9C: Apple Inc. states that Item 9C, “Disclosure Regarding Foreign Jurisdictions that Prevent Inspections,” is not applicable in its 2022 Form 10-K. The section serves as a brief transition into Part III of the annual report and contains no additional substantive disclosure, metrics, or named regulators.
sections.Item 10: Apple Inc. states that the information required by Item 10, including directors, executive officers, and corporate governance matters, will be provided in its definitive proxy statement. The company says this proxy statement will be filed with the SEC within 120 days after September 24, 2022, in connection with the solicitation of proxies for Apple’s 2023 annual meeting of shareholders, and is incorporated by reference into the 10-K.
sections.Item 11: Apple Inc. states that the executive compensation information required by Item 11 will be provided in its 2023 Proxy Statement and is incorporated into the 2022 Form 10-K by reference. This section contains no substantive compensation details in the filing itself, only a cross-reference to the forthcoming proxy statement.
sections.Item 12: Apple Inc. states that the information required by Item 12, including security ownership of certain beneficial owners and management and related stockholder matters, will be provided in the 2023 Proxy Statement and is incorporated into the 2022 Form 10-K by reference. This section contains no additional ownership data, metrics, or named beneficial owners beyond the cross-reference to the forthcoming proxy filing.
sections.Item 13: Apple Inc. states that the information required for Item 13, “Certain Relationships and Related Transactions, and Director Independence,” is not presented in the 2022 Form 10-K itself. Instead, Apple incorporates that disclosure by reference to its 2023 Proxy Statement, which will contain the relevant details on related-party transactions and director independence.
sections.Item 15: Item 15 of Apple Inc.’s 2022 Form 10-K provides the index to the consolidated financial statements for fiscal years ended September 24, 2022, September 25, 2021, and September 26, 2020, including the statements of operations, comprehensive income, balance sheets, shareholders’ equity, cash flows, notes, and the report of independent registered public accounting firm Ernst & Young LLP (PCAOB Firm ID No. 00042). It also states that all financial statement schedules are omitted as not applicable or already included in the consolidated financial statements and notes, and begins the Item 601 of Regulation S-K exhibit list with Apple’s restated articles of incorporation, amended bylaws, securities description, and debt indenture documents.
sections.Item 6: Item 6 of Apple Inc.’s 2022 Form 10-K is reserved and contains no substantive disclosure, with the section text shown only as “[Reserved].” This section does not provide additional financial, operational, regulatory, or product information beyond the filing identifier and page reference.
sections.Item 16: Item 16 contains Apple Inc.’s 2022 Form 10-K signatures and power-of-attorney authorization under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, confirming the report was signed on October 27, 2022 and filed with the SEC. Key named executives and directors include CEO and Principal Executive Officer Timothy D. Cook, CFO Luca Maestri, Principal Accounting Officer Chris Kondo, and directors James A. Bell, Al Gore, Alex Gorsky, Andrea Jung, Arthur D. Levinson, Monica Lozano, Ronald D. Sugar, and Susan L. Wagner.
sections.Item 14: Apple Inc. states that the information required by Item 14, Principal Accountant Fees and Services, will be included in its 2023 Proxy Statement and is incorporated by reference into the 2022 Form 10-K. This section appears in Part IV of Apple’s annual report filed with the SEC on October 28, 2022, and does not provide the fee details directly in the filing excerpt.

AAPL_10K_2023Q3_2023-11-03_full.txt.document_context: Apple Inc. filed a 10-K annual report for the fiscal year ended 2023-09-30. The company designs and sells iPhone, Mac, iPad, Wearables, Home and Accessories, and services such as the App Store, AppleCare, cloud services, and subscriptions including Apple Music, Apple Fitness+, and Apple News+.

sections.Item 1: Apple Inc. designs, manufactures, and markets a broad ecosystem of hardware and services, including iPhone, Mac, iPad, Apple Watch, AirPods, Apple TV, HomePod, AppleCare, cloud services, and digital content platforms such as the App Store, Apple Arcade, Apple Fitness+, Apple Music, and Apple News+. The section highlights major product lines and operating systems—iOS, macOS, iPadOS, watchOS, and tvOS—along with specific models like iPhone 15 Pro, MacBook Air, iPad Pro, and Apple Watch Ultra 2, emphasizing Apple’s integrated device-and-services strategy.
sections.Preamble: Apple Inc. filed its Form 10-K annual report for the fiscal year ended September 30, 2023 with the SEC under Commission File Number 001-36743, identifying itself as a California corporation headquartered at One Apple Park Way, Cupertino, California 95014. The filing lists Apple’s common stock (AAPL) on The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC and multiple outstanding debt securities, including notes due 2024, 2025, 2026, 2027, 2029, 2031, and 2042, and indicates Apple is a well-known seasoned issuer that has timely filed required Exchange Act reports and interactive data files.
sections.Item 1A: Apple Inc. says its business, reputation, results of operations, financial condition, and stock price are exposed to numerous known and unknown risks, and that past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. The section emphasizes macroeconomic and industry risks, noting that sales outside the U.S. represent a majority of total net sales and that Apple’s global supply chain is large and complex, with most supplier facilities and manufacturing/assembly sites located outside the U.S. Adverse conditions such as recession, inflation, tighter credit, higher interest rates, currency fluctuations, and weak consumer confidence could reduce demand and disrupt suppliers, contract manufacturers, logistics providers, distributors, cellular carriers, and other channel partners.
sections.Item 1B: Apple Inc. reported no unresolved staff comments in Item 1B of its 2023 Form 10-K annual report filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on November 3, 2023. This section indicates that there were no outstanding comments from SEC staff requiring resolution at the time of filing.
sections.Item 1C: Apple Inc.’s 2023 Form 10-K Item 1C states “Cybersecurity — Not applicable,” indicating the company did not provide a separate cybersecurity disclosure section in this filing. This section contains no additional operational, risk, incident, or regulatory details beyond the filing identification for Apple Inc. (AAPL).
sections.Item 2: Apple Inc. states that its headquarters is in Cupertino, California, and that as of September 30, 2023 it owned or leased facilities and land in the U.S. and internationally for corporate functions, research and development, data centers, retail, and other uses. The company says these facilities and equipment are used by all reportable segments, are in good operating condition, and are suitable for conducting its business.
sections.Item 4: Apple Inc. states that Item 4, Mine Safety Disclosures, is not applicable in its 2023 Form 10-K filed on November 3, 2023. The section contains no mine safety metrics, incidents, or regulatory disclosures, reflecting that Apple does not operate mining activities subject to Mine Safety and Health Administration reporting requirements.
sections.Item 6: Item 6 of Apple Inc.’s 2023 Form 10-K is reserved and contains no substantive disclosure, with the section text showing only “[Reserved]” and the page reference “Apple Inc. | 2023 Form 10-K | 19.” This section does not provide any financial metrics, business updates, products, or regulatory information.
sections.Item 7A: Apple Inc. says it is exposed to market risk from U.S. interest rates and foreign exchange rates, and uses derivatives, offsetting positions, duration management, and local-currency pricing to mitigate those risks. As of September 30, 2023, a hypothetical 100 basis point rise in rates would reduce the fair value of Apple’s investment portfolio by $3.089 billion and increase annual interest expense on term debt by $194 million, versus $4.022 billion and $201 million, respectively, in 2022. Apple also notes that a stronger U.S. dollar can hurt net sales and gross margins, and it uses a Monte Carlo simulation-based value-at-risk model to measure potential losses on foreign currency derivative positions.
sections.Item 3: Apple Inc. describes two major legal proceedings: Epic Games’ antitrust and California unfair competition lawsuit over the App Store in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California, where Apple won nine of ten counts but was enjoined from blocking external purchase links in iOS and iPadOS App Store apps, with the Ninth Circuit affirming and Apple seeking Supreme Court review. The section also covers Masimo Corporation and Cercacor Laboratories’ ITC patent complaint over Apple Watch blood oxygen functionality, resulting in an October 26, 2023 limited exclusion order affecting Apple Watch Series 9 and Ultra 2 imports and U.S. sales, plus a brief note that Apple faces other ordinary-course legal claims and settled certain nonmaterial matters in Q4 2023.
sections.Item 7: Apple Inc.’s Item 7 MD&A for fiscal 2023 compares 2023 with 2022, noting that the company’s 53-week fiscal year ended in September and that total net sales were $383.3 billion with net income of $97.0 billion. Revenue fell 3% year over year to $383.3 billion, with foreign-currency weakness versus the U.S. dollar driving the decline, lower Mac and iPhone sales partly offset by higher Services sales, and major product and software launches including iPad, MacBook Pro, Mac mini, HomePod, MacBook Air, Mac Studio, Mac Pro, Apple Vision Pro with visionOS, iOS 17/macOS Sonoma/iPadOS 17/tvOS 17/watchOS 10, iPhone 15, and Apple Watch Series 9/Ultra 2. Apple also announced a new $90 billion share repurchase authorization, raised its quarterly dividend to $0.24
sections.Item 9: Apple Inc. reported no changes in accountants and no disagreements with its accountants on accounting or financial disclosure matters in Item 9 of its 2023 Form 10-K. The section states “None,” indicating no relevant disputes, resignations, dismissals, or disclosure issues involving Apple’s independent auditors.
sections.Item 8: Item 8 presents Apple Inc.’s consolidated financial statements and notes for fiscal years ended September 30, 2023, September 24, 2022, and September 25, 2021, including statements of operations, comprehensive income, balance sheets, shareholders’ equity, and cash flows, with reports from the independent registered public accounting firm. The excerpt highlights Apple’s 2023 results: total net sales of $383.3 billion, gross margin of $169.1 billion, operating income of $114.3 billion, net income of $97.0 billion, and diluted EPS of $6.13, with revenue split between Products ($298.1 billion) and Services ($85.2 billion).
sections.Item 9B: Apple Inc. disclosed that on August 30, 2023, Senior Vice President, Retail Deirdre O’Brien and Chief Operating Officer Jeff Williams each adopted a trading plan intended to satisfy the affirmative defense conditions of Rule 10b5-1(c) under the Exchange Act. The plans provide for the sale of all shares vested during their terms from certain equity awards, excluding shares withheld for income tax withholding and remittance, and are set to expire on October 15, 2024 for O’Brien and December 15, 2024 for Williams, subject to early termination for specified events.
sections.Item 9A: Apple Inc. states that, as of September 30, 2023, its disclosure controls and procedures were effective under Exchange Act Rules 13a-15(e) and 15d-15(e), with the principal executive officer and principal financial officer concluding they provide reasonable assurance that required SEC disclosures are recorded, processed, summarized, reported on time, and communicated to management. The section also explains the inherent limitations of internal control over financial reporting under GAAP, noting that Apple’s controls are designed to maintain accurate records, ensure authorized transactions, and prevent or detect unauthorized asset use, but cannot provide absolute assurance against all errors or fraud.
sections.Item 9C: Apple Inc. states that Item 9C, “Disclosure Regarding Foreign Jurisdictions that Prevent Inspections,” is not applicable in this 2023 Form 10-K. The section serves as a brief transition into Part III of the annual report and contains no additional substantive disclosure, metrics, or named regulators beyond Apple Inc.
sections.Item 10: Apple Inc. states that the information required by Item 10, including directors, executive officers, and corporate governance matters, will be provided in its definitive proxy statement for the 2024 annual meeting of shareholders. The company says this 2024 Proxy Statement will be filed with the SEC within 120 days after September 30, 2023, and is incorporated into the 10-K by reference.
sections.Item 11: Apple Inc. states that the executive compensation information required by Item 11 will be provided in its 2024 Proxy Statement and is incorporated into the 10-K by reference. This section contains no compensation figures or named executives, only a cross-reference to the forthcoming proxy disclosure.
sections.Item 13: Apple Inc. states that the information required by Item 13, “Certain Relationships and Related Transactions, and Director Independence,” will be provided in the 2024 Proxy Statement and is incorporated into the 2023 Form 10-K by reference. This section contains no substantive disclosure beyond directing readers to the forthcoming Apple proxy materials for related-party transaction and director independence information.
sections.Item 12: Apple Inc. states that the information required by Item 12 on security ownership of certain beneficial owners and management, and related stockholder matters, will be provided in the 2024 Proxy Statement and is incorporated into the 2023 Form 10-K by reference. This section contains no additional ownership data, metrics, or named beneficial owners in the filing excerpt itself.
sections.Item 14: Apple Inc. states that the disclosure required by Item 14, Principal Accountant Fees and Services, will be included in the 2024 Proxy Statement and is incorporated by reference into the 2023 Form 10-K. This section appears in Part IV of Apple’s annual report and does not provide the fee details in the filing itself.
sections.Item 15: Item 15 of Apple Inc.’s 2023 Form 10-K lists the consolidated financial statements for fiscal years ended September 30, 2023, September 24, 2022, and September 25, 2021, including the statements of operations, comprehensive income, balance sheets, shareholders’ equity, cash flows, and notes, with the independent audit report by Ernst & Young LLP (PCAOB Firm ID No. 00042). It also states that all financial statement schedules were omitted as not applicable or already covered in the consolidated statements and notes, and provides an Item 601 Regulation S-K exhibit index covering Apple’s restated articles of incorporation, amended bylaws, securities description, and debt indenture and note certificates involving The Bank of New York Mellon Trust Company, N.A.
sections.Item 16: Item 16 contains Apple Inc.’s Form 10-K signatures and power-of-attorney authorizations under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, confirming the report was signed on November 2, 2023 and filed on Apple’s behalf. Named signatories include CEO and Director Timothy D. Cook, CFO Luca Maestri, Principal Accounting Officer Chris Kondo, and directors James A. Bell, Al Gore, Alex Gorsky, Andrea Jung, Arthur D. Levinson, Monica Lozano, Ronald D. Sugar, and Susan L. Wagner, with Luca Maestri signing for the company and Cook and Maestri appointed as attorneys-in-fact for SEC filings.
sections.Item 5: Apple Inc.’s common stock trades on The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC under the symbol AAPL, and as of October 20, 2023 the company had 23,763 shareholders of record. In the quarter ended September 30, 2023, Apple repurchased 106.595 million shares at an average price of $74.069 billion total value shown in the table, including open market and privately negotiated purchases and August 2023 accelerated share repurchase agreements (ASRs) with two financial institutions. As of September 30, 2023, Apple’s Board had authorized up to $90 billion of common stock repurchases under the May 4, 2023 program, with $15.9 billion utilized, and the company also used the final $4.6 billion under its prior April 2022 authorization.

AAPL_10K_2024Q3_2024-11-01_full.txt.document_context: Apple Inc. filed its 10-K annual report for the fiscal year ended 2024-09-28. The company designs and sells iPhone, Mac, iPad, Wearables, Home and Accessories, and Services, including AppleCare, Cloud Services, and digital content platforms such as the App Store, Apple Arcade, Apple Fitness+, and Apple Music.

sections.Item 1B: Apple Inc. states that there were no unresolved staff comments in Item 1B of its 2024 Form 10-K annual report filed with the SEC on November 1, 2024. This section indicates that the company had no outstanding comments from the Securities and Exchange Commission staff requiring further resolution.
sections.Item 1: Apple Inc. designs, manufactures, and markets a broad ecosystem of hardware and services, including iPhone smartphones running iOS, Mac personal computers running macOS, iPad tablets running iPadOS, and wearables, home, and accessories such as Apple Watch, AirPods, Beats, Apple Vision Pro, Apple TV, and HomePod. Its services portfolio includes advertising, AppleCare support, cloud services, and digital content platforms like the App Store, Apple Arcade, Apple Fitness+, and Apple Music, with the company operating on a 52- or 53-week fiscal year ending on the last Saturday of September.
sections.Preamble: Apple Inc. filed its Form 10-K annual report for the fiscal year ended September 28, 2024, under Commission File Number 001-36743, identifying itself as a California corporation headquartered at One Apple Park Way, Cupertino, California 95014, with telephone number (408) 996-1010 and IRS Employer Identification No. 94-2404110. The filing lists Apple’s common stock (AAPL) and multiple Nasdaq-listed debt securities, including notes due 2025, 2026, 2027, 2029, 2031, and 2042, and states that Apple is a well-known seasoned issuer, has filed all required Exchange Act reports and Interactive Data Files, and has no securities registered under Section 12(g).
sections.Item 1A: Apple Inc. says its business, reputation, results of operations, financial condition, and stock price are exposed to numerous known and unknown risks, and that past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. The excerpt emphasizes macroeconomic and industry risks: Apple depends heavily on global and regional economic conditions, with sales outside the U.S. representing a majority of total net sales and a large, complex global supply chain in which most supplier facilities and manufacturing/assembly sites are located outside the U.S. Adverse conditions such as recession, inflation, tighter credit, higher interest rates, currency fluctuations, and weak consumer confidence could reduce demand for Apple products and services and also strain suppliers, contract manufacturers, logistics providers, distributors, cellular carriers, and other channel partners.
sections.Item 2: Apple Inc. states that its headquarters is in Cupertino, California, and that as of September 28, 2024 it owned or leased facilities and land in the U.S. and internationally for corporate functions, research and development, data centers, retail, and other uses. The company says these facilities and equipment are used across all reportable segments, are in good operating condition, and are suitable for conducting its business.
sections.Item 4: Apple Inc. states that Item 4, Mine Safety Disclosures, is not applicable in its 2024 Form 10-K filed on November 1, 2024. The section contains no mine safety incidents, regulatory disclosures, or related metrics, reflecting that Apple does not operate mining activities subject to Mine Safety and Health Administration reporting.
sections.Item 1C: Apple Inc. says its management, led by the Head of Corporate Information Security, is responsible for identifying, assessing, and managing material cybersecurity risk through a dedicated Information Security team that sets policies and standards, trains employees, implements controls, monitors and responds to incidents, and performs security testing and assessments. The company also uses a Supplier Trust team for supplier-related risks, coordinates incident logging and escalation to senior management and the Audit and Finance Committee of the Board, and relies on the Audit Committee to oversee cybersecurity matters with regular input from the Head of Corporate Information Security, General Counsel, and compliance, assurance, and internal audit leaders.
sections.Item 6: Item 6 of Apple Inc.’s 2024 Form 10-K is reserved and contains no substantive disclosure, financial discussion, or operational information. The section appears only as a placeholder in the annual report filed with the SEC on November 1, 2024.
sections.Item 3: Apple Inc. reports multiple major legal proceedings centered on App Store and iOS platform rules, including two formal noncompliance investigations by the European Commission under the EU Digital Markets Act (DMA) and a third DMA investigation into contractual requirements for third-party app developers and app marketplaces. The Commission’s preliminary findings allege Apple’s App Store rules breach Article 5(4), and any final DMA violation could lead to cease-and-desist relief and fines of up to 10% of annual worldwide net sales, while Apple says it believes it complies and is adjusting its compliance plan. In the U.S., the DOJ and state and district attorneys general filed a civil antitrust suit in the U.S. District Court for the District of New Jersey alleging monopolization in “performance smartphones” and “smartphones,” and Epic Games, Inc. continues litigation in the Northern District of California over App Store antitrust and unfair competition claims.
sections.Item 5: Apple Inc.’s common stock trades on The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC under the symbol AAPL, and as of October 18, 2024 it had 23,301 shareholders of record. In the three months ended September 28, 2024, Apple repurchased 112.260 million shares at an average price of about $223 per share, with $89.074 billion remaining available under its repurchase programs; the Board had authorized up to $110 billion under the May 2, 2024 program, of which $20.9 billion had been used, and Apple also completed the final $4.1 billion under its prior May 2023 authorization. The section also notes Apple’s five-year cumulative total shareholder return comparison against the S&P 500 Index and the Dow Jones U.S. Technology Supersector Index.
sections.Item 7: Apple Inc.’s Item 7 MD&A in the 2024 Form 10-K discusses fiscal 2024 and 2023 results, product and software launches, fiscal-year timing, macroeconomic pressures, and segment net sales trends, with comparisons to 2023 and references to prior-year context in the 2023 Form 10-K. Key 2024 announcements included MacBook Pro and MacBook Air models, iMac, iPad Air and iPad Pro, iOS 18/macOS Sequoia/iPadOS 18/watchOS 11/visionOS 2/tvOS 18, Apple Intelligence™, iPhone 16 family, Apple Watch Series 10, and AirPods 4, while net sales totaled $391.035 billion, led by Americas ($167.045 billion) and Europe ($101.328 billion) and offset by a decline in Greater China to $66.952 billion.
sections.Item 9: Apple Inc. reported no changes in accountants and no disagreements with accountants on accounting or financial disclosure for the 2024 Form 10-K. The Item 9 section states “None,” indicating no disputes or related issues to disclose.
sections.Item 8: Item 8 presents Apple Inc.’s consolidated financial statements and notes for fiscal years ended September 28, 2024, September 30, 2023, and September 24, 2022, including the statements of operations, comprehensive income, balance sheets, shareholders’ equity, cash flows, and the report of the independent registered public accounting firm. Apple reported 2024 net sales of $391.0 billion, gross margin of $180.7 billion, operating income of $123.2 billion, and net income of $93.7 billion, with Products sales of $294.9 billion and Services sales of $96.2 billion.
sections.Item 7A: Apple Inc. says it is exposed to market risk from U.S. interest rates and foreign exchange rates, and uses derivatives, offsetting positions, and portfolio duration management to mitigate those risks. As of September 28, 2024, a hypothetical 100 basis point rise in rates would reduce the fair value of Apple’s investment portfolio by $2,755 million and increase annual interest expense on term debt by $139 million, versus $3,089 million and $194 million, respectively, in 2023. For currency risk, Apple notes it is a net receiver of non-U.S. dollar currencies, that a stronger U.S. dollar can hurt net sales and gross margins, and that it uses a Monte Carlo-based value-at-risk model for foreign currency derivative positions.
sections.Item 9A: Apple Inc. states that, as of September 28, 2024, its disclosure controls and procedures were effective under Exchange Act Rules 13a-15(e) and 15d-15(e), providing reasonable assurance that required SEC disclosures are recorded, processed, summarized, reported on time, and communicated to management, including the principal executive officer and principal financial officer. The section also explains that Apple’s internal control over financial reporting is designed to provide reasonable assurance for GAAP-compliant financial statements, accurate asset records, authorized transactions, and prevention or timely detection of unauthorized asset use, while emphasizing inherent limitations, resource constraints, and that controls cannot prevent or detect all errors or fraud.
sections.Item 9C: Apple Inc. states that Item 9C, “Disclosure Regarding Foreign Jurisdictions that Prevent Inspections,” is not applicable in this Form 10-K. The section serves as a brief transition into Part III of the annual report and contains no additional substantive disclosure, metrics, or named regulators.
sections.Item 9B: Apple Inc. disclosed new Rule 10b5-1 trading plans under the Exchange Act for two executives: Deirdre O’Brien, Senior Vice President, Retail, and Jeff Williams, Chief Operating Officer. O’Brien’s August 27, 2024 plan covers sales of vested equity-award shares from April 1, 2025 through October 1, 2026 and expires December 31, 2026, while Williams’ August 29, 2024 plan covers up to 100,000 shares of common stock plus vested equity-award shares from April 1, 2025 through October 1, 2025 and expires December 15, 2025, with both plans subject to price limits and excluding shares withheld for tax obligations.
sections.Item 11: Apple Inc. states that the executive compensation information required by Item 11 is not included in this 10-K and will instead be provided in the 2025 Proxy Statement, which is incorporated herein by reference. This section contains no compensation figures, named executives, or additional details beyond the cross-reference to the forthcoming proxy disclosure.
sections.Item 10: Apple Inc. states that it maintains an insider trading policy covering the purchase, sale, and other dispositions of its securities by directors, officers, employees, and other covered persons, along with procedures for repurchases of its securities. The company says these policies are reasonably designed to promote compliance with insider trading laws, SEC rules and regulations, and applicable listing standards, and notes that the policy is filed as Exhibit 19.1 to the Form 10-K. Remaining Item 10 disclosures will be provided in Apple’s definitive proxy statement for the 2025 annual meeting of shareholders, to be filed with the SEC within 120 days after September 28, 2024.

The excerpt says Apple designs and sells iPhone, Mac, iPad, wearables, and services, including AppleCare, Cloud Services, and digital content platforms such as the App Store, Apple Arcade, Apple Fitness+, and Apple Music.

sections.Item 1B: Apple Inc.’s Item 1B, Unresolved Staff Comments, states that there were no unresolved comments from the SEC staff as of the 2025-10-31 Form 10-K filing. This section indicates that Apple had no outstanding regulatory review issues requiring disclosure in this annual report.
sections.Item 1: Apple Inc. designs, manufactures and markets iPhone smartphones, Mac personal computers, iPad tablets, Apple Watch wearables, AirPods/Beats audio products, Apple Vision Pro spatial computers, and home devices such as Apple TV 4K and HomePod, with product lines built on iOS, macOS, iPadOS, watchOS, visionOS and tvOS. The Company also sells services including advertising, AppleCare support, cloud services, and digital content through the App Store, Apple Arcade, Apple Fitness+, Apple Music and other subscription offerings, and reports on a 52- or 53-week fiscal year ending on the last Saturday of September.
sections.Preamble: Apple Inc. filed this Form 10-K annual report with the SEC under Commission File Number 001-36743 for the fiscal year ended September 27, 2025, identifying its principal executive offices at One Apple Park Way, Cupertino, California 95014 and its employer identification number as 94-2404110. The filing lists Apple’s common stock (AAPL) and multiple Nasdaq-listed debt securities, including 0.000% Notes due 2025, 1.625% Notes due 2026, 2.000% Notes due 2027, 1.375% Notes due 2029, 3.050% Notes due 2029, 0.500% Notes due 2031, and 3.600% Notes due 2042, and indicates Apple is a well-known seasoned issuer that has timely filed required reports and interactive data files under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.
sections.Item 1A: Apple Inc. says its business, results of operations, financial condition, reputation, and stock price could be materially harmed by risks it may not be able to predict, control, or mitigate, and that the Item 1A list is not exhaustive. The excerpt emphasizes macroeconomic and industry risks: Apple has majority of net sales outside the U.S., a large and complex global supply chain with most supplier facilities outside the U.S., and its demand is sensitive to recession, unemployment, inflation, tighter credit, higher interest rates, currency fluctuations, and changes in fiscal and monetary policy. It also warns that weak global conditions could impair suppliers, contract manufacturers, logistics providers, distributors, cellular network carriers, and developers through financial instability, reduced access to credit, or insolvency.
sections.Item 2: Apple Inc. states that its headquarters is in Cupertino, California, and that as of September 27, 2025 it owned or leased facilities and land for corporate functions, research and development, data centers, retail, and other purposes across the U.S. and internationally. The company says these facilities and equipment are used by all reportable segments and are in good operating condition and suitable for conducting its business.
sections.Item 1C: Apple Inc. says its management, led by the Head of Corporate Information Security, has overall responsibility for identifying, assessing, and managing material cybersecurity risks through a dedicated Information Security team that sets policies, trains employees, implements controls, monitors and responds to incidents, and conducts security testing and assessments. The company also uses a Supplier Trust team to manage supplier-related information security risk and maintains processes to log, track, escalate, and report cybersecurity incidents to senior management and the Audit and Finance Committee of the Board, while the Audit Committee oversees cybersecurity matters and regularly reviews risks with management, including the General Counsel and heads of Compliance and Business Conduct, Business Assurance, and Internal Audit.
sections.Item 5: Apple Inc.’s common stock trades on The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC under the symbol AAPL, and as of October 17, 2025 the company had 22,429 shareholders of record. During the three months ended September 27, 2025, Apple repurchased 89.498 million shares at an average price of $222.75 per share, leaving $99.779 billion available under its buyback programs, including the May 2, 2024 authorization for up to $110 billion and the May 1, 2025 authorization for up to $100 billion.
sections.Item 3: Apple’s Item 3 describes major antitrust and regulatory litigation, led by European Commission Digital Markets Act investigations under Article 5(4) and Article 6(4) concerning App Store steering, developer communications, and contractual terms for third-party app developers and app marketplaces. The Commission fined Apple €500 million on April 23, 2025 and ordered it to remove restrictions, while Apple has appealed and says it complies with the DMA; the section also notes a March 21, 2024 DOJ and state attorneys general lawsuit in the U.S. District Court for the District of New Jersey alleging monopolization in “performance smartphones” and “smartphones,” plus related civil suits and the Epic Games litigation in the Northern District of California.
sections.Item 6: Item 6 of Apple Inc.’s 2025 Form 10-K is reserved and contains no substantive disclosure, with the section text limited to a placeholder “[Reserved]” in the annual report filed with the SEC. This section does not provide financial metrics, business updates, or other material information beyond the filing identifier and page reference.
sections.Item 9: Apple Inc. reported no changes in accountants and no disagreements with its accountants on accounting or financial disclosure for the period covered by this Form 10-K. sections.Item 7: Apple Inc.’s Item 7 MD&A for fiscal 2025 and 2024 highlights year-to-year operating trends alongside major product, service, and software launches, including MacBook Pro, Mac mini, iMac, iPad mini, iPhone 16e, iPad Air, MacBook Air, Mac Studio, iOS 26, macOS Tahoe 26, iPadOS 26, watchOS 26, visionOS 26, tvOS 26, iPhone 17, iPhone Air, iPhone 17 Pro, iPhone 17 Pro Max, Apple Watch Series 11, Apple Watch SE 3, Apple Watch Ultra 3, and AirPods Pro 3. The section also notes Apple’s 52- or 53-week fiscal calendar, with fiscal 2025 and 2024 each spanning 52 weeks, and warns that inflation, interest rates, currency fluctuations, and new U.S. tariffs announced in 202
sections.Item 4: Apple Inc. states that Item 4, Mine Safety Disclosures, is not applicable in its 2025 Form 10-K. The section contains no mine safety incidents, citations, or regulatory disclosures from the Mine Safety and Health Administration (MSHA), reflecting that Apple’s business does not involve mining operations.
sections.Item 9C: Apple Inc. states that Item 9C, “Disclosure Regarding Foreign Jurisdictions that Prevent Inspections,” is not applicable in this Form 10-K. The section is a brief transition into Part III of the annual report and contains no additional substantive disclosure, metrics, or named regulators beyond Apple Inc.
sections.Item 7A: Apple Inc. says it is exposed to market risk from U.S. interest rates and foreign exchange rates, and uses derivatives, offsetting positions, duration management, and local-currency pricing to mitigate those risks. As of September 27, 2025, a hypothetical 100 basis point rise in rates would reduce the fair value of Apple’s investment portfolio by $2.416 billion and increase annual interest expense on term debt by $129 million, versus $2.755 billion and $139 million, respectively, in 2024. For currency risk, Apple notes it is a net receiver of non-U.S. dollar currencies and uses a Monte Carlo-based value-at-risk model to measure potential losses on foreign currency derivative positions.
sections.Item 9B: On October 31, 2025, Apple Inc. announced a finance leadership transition in which Chris Kondo, Senior Director of Corporate Accounting and Principal Accounting Officer, will step down from that role effective January 1, 2026 and continue working on other projects. Ben Borders, Apple’s Director of Technical Accounting, will succeed him as Senior Director of Corporate Accounting and Principal Accounting Officer, reporting to Chief Financial Officer Kevan Parekh; the section also states that there were no insider trading arrangements.
sections.Item 8: Item 8 presents Apple Inc.’s consolidated financial statements and notes for fiscal years ended September 27, 2025, September 28, 2024, and September 30, 2023, including the statements of operations, comprehensive income, balance sheets, shareholders’ equity, and cash flows, along with the report of the independent registered public accounting firm. Apple reported total net sales of $416.161 billion in 2025 versus $391.035 billion in 2024 and $383.285 billion in 2023, with products sales of $307.003 billion and services revenue of $109.158 billion in 2025. Net income rose to $112.010 billion in 2025, with diluted EPS of $7.46, operating income of $133.050 billion, and research and development expense of $34.550 billion.
sections.Item 9A: Apple Inc. states that, based on management’s evaluation under the supervision of its principal executive officer and principal financial officer, its disclosure controls and procedures were effective as of September 27, 2025 under Exchange Act Rules 13a-15(e) and 15d-15(e), providing reasonable assurance that required SEC disclosures are recorded, processed, summarized, reported, and communicated on time. The section also explains that Apple’s internal control over financial reporting is designed to provide reasonable assurance under GAAP for accurate records, authorized transactions, and protection of assets, but that even a well-designed control system cannot provide absolute assurance or prevent or detect all errors and fraud.
sections.Item 10: Apple Inc. states that the information required by Item 10, including directors, executive officers, and corporate governance matters, will be provided in its definitive proxy statement to be filed with the SEC within 120 days after September 27, 2025. sections.Item 3: Apple Inc. states that there were no material changes to its market risk during the first three months of 2026, and directs readers to Part II, Item 7A of its 2025 Form 10-K for a fuller discussion of market risk exposure. The section also reports no defaults upon senior securities.
sections.Item 1A: Apple Inc. states that its business, reputation, results of operations, financial condition, and stock price may be materially and adversely affected by a range of known or unknown risks, including those previously described in Part I, Item 1A of the 2025 Form 10-K under “Risk Factors.” The company reports that there have been no material changes to its risk factors since the 2025 Form 10-K.
sections.Item 4: Apple Inc. states that, as of December 27, 2025, its disclosure controls and procedures were effective under Exchange Act Rules 13a-15(e) and 15d-15(e), with the principal executive officer and principal financial officer concluding the controls provided reasonable assurance that required SEC information was recorded, processed, summarized, reported, and communicated on time. The company also reported no changes in internal control over financial reporting during the first quarter of 2026 that materially affected, or were reasonably likely to materially affect, internal control, and noted that Item 4 Mine Safety Disclosures were not applicable.
sections.Preamble: Apple Inc. filed this Form 10-Q quarterly report for the period ended December 27, 2025, under Commission File Number 001-36743, identifying its principal executive offices at One Apple Park Way, Cupertino, California 95014 and telephone number (408) 996-1010. The filing lists Apple’s common stock (AAPL) and multiple Nasdaq-listed debt securities, including 1.625% Notes due 2026, 2.000% Notes due 2027, 1.375% Notes due 2029, 3.050% Notes due 2029, 0.500% Notes due 2031, and 3.600% Notes due 2042, and states that Apple is a large accelerated filer that has filed all required Exchange Act reports and submitted all required Interactive Data Files.
sections.Item 2: Apple Inc.’s Item 2 discussion in this Form 10-Q emphasizes standard MD&A framing, including extensive forward-looking statement cautionary language under the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 and references to risks from macroeconomic conditions, tariffs, and other measures that could affect results of operations. It also notes that the filing should be read with Apple’s 2025 Form 10-K and the condensed consolidated financial statements, and points investors to Apple’s corporate website, www.apple.com, and investor relations site, investor.apple.com, for press releases, financial performance information, corporate governance materials, and annual meeting details, while stating that website content is not incorporated by reference.
sections.Item 6: Item 6 of Apple Inc.’s Q1 2026 Form 10-Q lists the filing’s exhibits, including CEO and CFO certifications under Rule 13a-14(a)/15d-14(a) (Exhibits 31.1 and 31.2), Section 1350 certifications (Exhibit 32.1), and Inline XBRL materials for the condensed consolidated financial statements and cover page (Exhibits 101 and 104). The section also includes the signature page showing the report was signed on January 30, 2026 by Kevan Parekh, Apple’s Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, pursuant to the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.
sections.Item 5: Apple Inc. disclosed insider trading arrangements under Rule 10b5-1(c) for Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer Kevan Parekh and Senior Vice President, Retail + People Deirdre O’Brien. Parekh adopted a plan on November 21, 2025 to sell vested equity-award shares from April 15, 2026 through October 15, 2026, while O’Brien terminated her prior August 27, 2024 plan on November 24, 2025 and entered a new plan the same day covering shares vesting from April 1, 2026 through October 1, 2026; both plans exclude shares withheld for tax obligations and are set to expire December 31, 2026.
sections.Item 1: Apple Inc.’s Q1 2026 unaudited condensed consolidated statements show total net sales of $143.756 billion, up from $124.300 billion a year earlier, driven by Products revenue of $113.743 billion and Services revenue of $30.013 billion. Gross margin was $69.231 billion, operating income was $50.852 billion, and net income was $42.097 billion, with diluted EPS of $2.84 versus $2.40 in the prior-year quarter. The section also reports comprehensive income items including foreign currency translation, derivative instruments, and marketable debt securities, alongside shares used in EPS calculations and income tax provision of $8.905 billion.

ABBV_10K_2025-02-14_full.txt.document_context: AbbVie Inc. filed a 10-K annual report covering the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024. The company said it uses derivative instruments to hedge foreign exchange and interest-rate exposure, with $15.237 billion of foreign currency forward contracts outstanding at December 31, 2024 and an estimated $1.5 billion decline in fair value if underlying currencies appreciated 10% against the U.S. dollar.

sections.Preamble: AbbVie Inc. (NYSE: ABBV), a Delaware corporation headquartered at 1 North Waukegan Road, North Chicago, Illinois, filed its Form 10-K annual report with the SEC for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 under Commission file number 001-35565. The preamble lists AbbVie’s registered securities, including common stock and senior notes due 2027, 2028, 2029, and 2031, and indicates the company is a well-known seasoned issuer, has filed all required Exchange Act reports and Interactive Data Files, and is a large accelerated filer.
sections.Item 7A: AbbVie Inc. says its earnings, cash flows, and equity are exposed to foreign exchange and interest rate risk, and it uses derivative instruments on a cost-effective basis to hedge underlying economic exposures, with additional detail referenced in Note 11 to the Consolidated Financial Statements. As of December 31, 2024, AbbVie’s primary net foreign currency exposures were the euro, Japanese yen, Canadian dollar, and British pound, with $15.237 billion of foreign currency forward exchange contracts outstanding (vs. $15.924 billion in 2023) and a total fair and carrying value receivable of $278 million; a 10% appreciation in hedged currencies would reduce the fair value of these contracts by $1.5 billion and negatively affect earnings over the contracts’ remaining life. AbbVie also had unsecured senior Euro notes outstanding and designated €3.1 billion of aggregate principal amount as hedges of net investments in certain foreign subsidiaries and affiliates to offset foreign currency translation

ADBE_10K_2026-01-15_full.txt.document_context: Adobe Inc. filed a 10-K annual report for its fiscal year ended 2025. The excerpt says Adobe’s mission is to empower everyone to create and that it is increasingly using AI across its products, including commercially safe first-party and partner models, conversational and agentic capabilities, and an end-to-end platform spanning ideation through activation.

sections.Item 1: Adobe Inc. says its mission is to empower everyone to create by providing end-to-end digital experience platforms and tools that support ideation, creation, production, and personalized delivery across media and channels for business professionals, consumers, creators, creative professionals, and marketing professionals. The company emphasizes its AI strategy and Adobe AI platform, combining first-party and partner AI models with conversational and agentic capabilities, trusted and secure workflows, brand safety, compliance, and intellectual property protection to scale creativity, productivity, and customer experience orchestration globally.
sections.Preamble: Adobe Inc. (NASDAQ: ADBE) filed its Form 10-K annual report under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 for the fiscal year ended November 28, 2025, identifying itself as a Delaware corporation with principal executive offices at 345 Park Avenue, San Jose, California 95110-2704 and telephone number (408) 536-6000. The filing states that Adobe has common stock registered under Section 12(b), no Section 12(g) securities, is a well-known seasoned issuer and large accelerated filer, and confirms it has timely filed required Exchange Act reports and submitted all required Interactive Data Files.
sections.Item 1A: Adobe Inc. says its results could differ materially from forward-looking statements if it fails to innovate quickly enough in rapidly evolving technology and creative markets, where developing new solutions requires significant investment, operational costs, and uncertain commercial success. The company highlights artificial intelligence, including generative and agentic AI, as a major opportunity and risk, noting that its new AI solutions and AI enhancements across existing products may not be successful, adopted, or monetized as customer needs, standards, platforms, and technologies continue to change.
sections.Item 1B: Adobe Inc. (ADBE) states that there were no unresolved staff comments in Item 1B of its 2026 10-K Annual Report filed with the SEC on January 15, 2026. This section indicates that the company had no outstanding comments from the Securities and Exchange Commission staff requiring resolution.
sections.Item 1C: Adobe Inc. describes an information security risk management framework for products, services, infrastructure, and corporate resources, overseen by a cybersecurity risk steering committee and supported by regular security reviews, simulations, internal and external penetration testing, vulnerability assessments, threat intelligence, and third-party cybersecurity consultants. The company also maintains annual employee security awareness training, a vendor security review program for third parties handling Adobe non-public data or connecting to its networks, and an incident response plan to monitor, analyze, escalate, and report cybersecurity incidents. Governance is led by the Board of Directors and the Audit Committee, which oversees enterprise and cybersecurity risks, information security controls, and receives regular cybersecurity updates.
sections.Item 4: Adobe Inc. (ADBE) states that Item 4, Mine Safety Disclosures, is not applicable in its 2026 Form 10-K annual report filed with the SEC on January 15, 2026. The section contains no mine safety incidents, citations, or related metrics, reflecting that Adobe is not engaged in mining operations subject to Mine Safety and Health Administration disclosure requirements.
sections.Item 3: Adobe Inc. incorporates by reference the “Legal Proceedings” disclosure in Note 16 of its Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements for its 2026 Form 10-K. This section indicates that the company’s material litigation and regulatory matters are described elsewhere in the filing rather than restated here.
sections.Item 2: Adobe Inc. states that its corporate headquarters is in San Jose, California, where it occupies approximately 1.7 million square feet of office space and owns all San Jose properties used for research, product development, sales, marketing, and administrative functions. The company also owns and leases facilities across the United States, Europe, Middle East and Africa (EMEA), and Asia-Pacific (APAC), including data centers, with its largest non-U.S. sites in Bangalore, India and Noida, India at about 0.7 million square feet each. Adobe says it operates under a hybrid work model and believes its owned and leased properties are in good operating condition and suitable for its business, with lease obligations discussed in Note 7 to the consolidated financial statements.
sections.Item 6: Item 6 of Adobe Inc.’s 2026 Form 10-K is reserved and contains no substantive disclosure, showing only a table of contents reference and page number 31. This section does not provide financial metrics, business updates, risk factors, or other named entities beyond Adobe Inc. and the SEC EDGAR filing structure.
sections.Item 7: Adobe Inc. says its Item 7 MD&A should be read with the Consolidated Financial Statements and Notes, and it highlights that revenue recognition and income taxes are its critical accounting policies under GAAP and SEC rules because they require significant assumptions, judgments, and estimates. The company notes these estimates are regularly reviewed with the Audit Committee of the Board of Directors and have historically not differed materially from actual results. In revenue recognition, Adobe emphasizes that certain subscription offerings combining on-premise/on-device software licenses with cloud services are treated as a single, highly integrated performance obligation due to the interdependency of the software and cloud components.
sections.Item 5: Adobe Inc. states that its common stock trades on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol “ADBE,” and that it had 801 holders of record as of January 9, 2026, though the company cannot estimate total beneficial ownership because many shares are held through brokers and institutions. Adobe does not anticipate paying cash dividends in the foreseeable future and continued an active share repurchase program, buying 7.2 million shares during the three months ended November 28, 2025 at average prices of $353.68, $345.96, and $332.31 per share, with $6.369 billion remaining under the Board’s March 2024 authorization to repurchase up to $25 billion through March 14, 2028.
sections.Item 7A: Adobe Inc. says all market-risk sensitive instruments are used for non-trading purposes, with foreign currency risk managed through foreign exchange forward and option contracts that hedge forecasted revenue and expenses as well as net recognized monetary assets and liabilities. As of November 28, 2025, Adobe had $6.54 billion of outstanding FX contracts expiring through September 2027, including exposures in euros, Japanese yen, British pounds, Indian rupees, Australian dollars, and Canadian dollars, and it reported that a 10% strengthening of the U.S. dollar would increase the fair value of its hedging instruments by $456 million. The company also notes counterparty credit risk is mitigated through master netting arrangements and collateral security agreements.
sections.Item 8: Adobe Inc.’s Item 8 presents the consolidated financial statements and notes for the fiscal years ended November 28, 2025 and November 29, 2024, including the balance sheets, income, comprehensive income, stockholders’ equity, and cash flow statements, with the audit report issued by KPMG LLP of Santa Clara, California (PCAOB ID 185). The consolidated balance sheets show total assets of $29.496 billion versus $30.230 billion a year earlier, driven by cash and cash equivalents of $5.431 billion, goodwill of $12.857 billion, deferred revenue of $6.905 billion, and total liabilities of $17.873 billion, including $6.210 billion of long-term debt and $15.361 billion of additional paid-in capital.
sections.Item 9: Adobe Inc. (ADBE) reported no changes in accountants and no disagreements with its independent accountants on accounting or financial disclosure matters in Item 9 of its 2026 Form 10-K. The section states “None,” indicating no disputes, restatements, or related issues were disclosed.
sections.Item 9B: Item 9B, Other Information, for Adobe Inc. (ADBE) states that there is no additional information to report in this section. The filing provides no new disclosures, events, or regulatory matters under Item 9B beyond “None.”
sections.Item 9C: Adobe Inc. (ADBE) states that Item 9C, “Disclosure Regarding Foreign Jurisdictions That Prevent Inspections,” is not applicable in this Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended 2026-01-15. The section appears as a brief Part III placeholder with no foreign jurisdiction inspection issues, regulatory findings, or related metrics disclosed.
sections.Item 10: Adobe Inc. states that most Item 10 disclosures on directors, executive officers, and corporate governance are incorporated by reference from its 2026 Proxy Statement, including director nominees for the 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, election of directors, corporate governance guidelines, codes of business conduct and ethics, and board committees, with the proxy to be filed with the SEC within 120 days after fiscal year-end. The section also describes Adobe’s insider trading policy, which applies to directors, officers, employees, subsidiaries, and Adobe itself, and is designed to promote compliance with insider trading laws, rules, regulations, and applicable listing standards; the policy is filed as Exhibit 19.1.
sections.Item 9A: Adobe Inc. reports that, as of November 28, 2025, its Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer concluded disclosure controls and procedures were effective at the reasonable assurance level under Exchange Act Rules 13a-15(e) and 15d-15(e), enabling timely recording, processing, summarization, and reporting of information required in the Form 10-K. Management also concluded that internal control over financial reporting was effective as of the same date based on the COSO Internal Control—Integrated Framework (2013), while noting the inherent limitations of any control system and that no system can provide absolute assurance against errors or fraud. KPMG LLP, Adobe’s independent registered public accounting firm, issued an attestation report on internal control over financial reporting.
sections.Item 11: Adobe Inc. states that the information required by Item 11 of Form 10-K is not presented in the annual report itself but is incorporated by reference from its 2026 Proxy Statement. The referenced proxy statement sections are “Executive Compensation,” “Corporate Governance,” and “Report of the Audit Committee,” indicating that executive pay and governance disclosures are deferred to that filing.
sections.Item 12: Adobe Inc. states that the information required by Item 12 of Form 10-K is not presented in the annual report itself but will be included by reference in its 2026 Proxy Statement under the captions “Security Ownership of Certain Beneficial Owners and Management” and “Equity Compensation Plan Information.” This section therefore points readers to the proxy statement for details on beneficial ownership, management ownership, and equity compensation plan information.
sections.Item 13: Adobe Inc. states that the information required for Item 13 of Form 10-K, covering certain relationships and related transactions and director independence, is not presented in the annual report itself but will be included by reference from the company’s 2026 Proxy Statement. The referenced proxy statement sections are “Certain Relationships and Related Persons Transactions” and “Director Independence,” and the filing incorporates that information herein by reference.
sections.Item 14: Adobe Inc. (ADBE) states that the information required by Item 14 of Form 10-K, “Principal Accounting Fees and Services,” is not presented in the annual report itself but will be included in Adobe’s 2026 Proxy Statement and incorporated by reference. This section is a brief cross-reference within Part IV of the 2026 Annual Report filed with the SEC, with no fee amounts, auditor names, or service details disclosed in the excerpt.
sections.Item 15: Item 15 lists Adobe Inc.’s exhibits and financial statement schedules for its 2026 Form 10-K, including the reference to the consolidated financial statements in Part II, Item 8. The exhibit index highlights the 2022 Agreement and Plan of Merger with Figma, Inc. and related merger entities, Adobe’s restated charter and amended bylaws, multiple debt indentures and global notes with Wells Fargo Bank, National Association and Computershare Trust Company, N.A. as trustees, and securities descriptions and equity compensation plans such as the 2020 Employee Stock Purchase Plan, 2019 Equity Incentive Plan, and 2023 Performance Share Program.
sections.Item 16: Item 16 contains Adobe Inc.’s 10-K signatures and power of attorney, showing the report was duly authorized under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and filed on January 15, 2026. Key signatories include Shantanu Narayen (Chair and CEO), Daniel Durn (Chief Financial Officer and Executive Vice President, Finance, Technology, Security and Operations), and Jillian Forusz (Chief Accounting Officer and Corporate Controller), along with directors such as Frank Calderoni, Cristiano Amon, Amy Banse, Melanie Boulden, Laura Desmond, Spencer Neumann, Kathleen Oberg, Dheeraj Pandey, David Ricks, and Dan Rosensweig.

AMZN_10K_2022Q4_2023-02-03_full.txt.document_context: Amazon.com Inc. filed a 10-K annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2022. The excerpt says Amazon operates in three segments—North America, International, and AWS—and serves consumers, sellers, developers, enterprises, content creators, advertisers, and employees. It also highlights Amazon Prime and its fulfillment network, with hundreds of millions of products sold across online and physical stores.

sections.Item 1B: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) states in Item 1B of its 2023 Form 10-K that there were no unresolved staff comments from the SEC. This section indicates that the company had no outstanding issues or questions pending resolution with the Securities and Exchange Commission staff as of the filing date.
sections.Preamble: Amazon.com, Inc. filed its Form 10-K annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2022 with the SEC under Commission File No. 000-22513, identifying itself as a Delaware corporation headquartered at 410 Terry Avenue North, Seattle, Washington 98109-5210, with common stock trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol AMZN. The filing states that Amazon is a well-known seasoned issuer and a large accelerated filer, has complied with Exchange Act reporting and Interactive Data File submission requirements over the prior 12 months, and has no securities registered under Section 12(g).
sections.Item 1A: Amazon.com Inc. says its businesses face intense and rapidly evolving competition across e-commerce, physical and omnichannel retail, e-commerce services, web and infrastructure computing services, electronic devices, digital content, advertising, grocery, and transportation and logistics, with rivals often having greater resources, longer histories, more customers, or stronger brand recognition. The company warns that competitors may undercut pricing, secure better vendor terms, and spend more on technology, infrastructure, fulfillment, and marketing, while new business models, alliances, and technologies such as search and cloud services further intensify pressure and could materially harm Amazon’s business, financial condition, cash flows, liquidity, and stock price.
sections.Item 5: Amazon.com Inc. common stock trades on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol “AMZN.” As of January 25, 2023, Amazon reported 10,845 shareholders of record, with a much larger number of beneficial owners, and stated that it had no recent sales of unregistered securities and no issuer purchases of equity securities.
sections.Item 4: Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) states that Item 4, Mine Safety Disclosures, is not applicable in its 2023 Form 10-K filed with the SEC on February 3, 2023. The section contains no mine safety operations, metrics, citations, or regulatory disclosures.
sections.Item 2: As of December 31, 2022, Amazon.com Inc. operated 617,483 thousand square feet of leased facilities and 43,927 thousand square feet of owned facilities worldwide, including office space, 611 North America and 32 international physical stores, and large fulfillment, data center, and other operations. By segment, Amazon reported 562,916 thousand square feet leased and 35,333 thousand square feet owned, with North America, International, and AWS comprising the principal property footprint; corporate headquarters are owned and leased in Washington’s Puget Sound region and Arlington, Virginia.
sections.Item 1: Amazon.com Inc. describes itself as an Earth’s most customer-centric company, organized into three operating segments—North America, International, and Amazon Web Services (AWS)—and serving consumers, sellers, developers, enterprises, content creators, advertisers, and employees. The business overview highlights its online and physical stores, third-party marketplace, fulfillment and customer service networks, and consumer devices and media offerings such as Kindle, Fire tablet, Fire TV, Echo, Ring, Blink, eero, and Amazon Prime, emphasizing low prices, fast and free delivery, and convenience.
sections.Item 6: Item 6 of Amazon.com, Inc.’s 2022 Form 10-K is marked “Reserved” and contains no substantive discussion, financial data, or business updates. The section appears only as a placeholder in the table of contents and does not add any material information beyond the filing structure.

sections.Item 7: Amazon.com Inc.’s Item 7 MD&A emphasizes that its 2023 Form 10-K contains extensive forward-looking statements and highlights major risk drivers including foreign exchange, inflation, interest rates, global economic and geopolitical conditions, customer demand, supply chain constraints, labor markets, litigation, acquisitions, and fulfillment and data center optimization. The section also states that Amazon’s primary revenue source is the sale of a wide range of products and services through its stores, including first-party merchandise, content, and third-party seller offerings, with risk disclosures cross-referenced to Item 1A “Risk Factors.”

sections.Item 7A: Amazon.com Inc. says it is exposed to market risk from interest rate changes, foreign currency fluctuations, and changes in the market values of its investments, with further discussion tied to Item 7 liquidity and capital resources. Its interest rate risk is concentrated in its investment portfolio and fixed-rate long-term debt, and as of December 31, 2022 it held $27.899 billion in money market funds plus marketable fixed income securities including $23.075 billion of corporate debt securities, $2.282 billion of U.S. government and agency securities, and $2.690 billion of asset-backed securities, all with disclosed weighted-average interest rates and estimated fair values.
sections.Item 8: Item 8 presents Amazon.com, Inc.’s consolidated financial statements and supplementary data for the years ended December 31, 2022 and 2021, including the consolidated statements of cash flows, operations, comprehensive income (loss), balance sheets, stockholders’ equity, and notes. Ernst & Young LLP, the independent registered public accounting firm (PCAOB ID: 42), audited the statements and Amazon’s internal control over financial reporting under PCAOB standards and the COSO 2013 framework, issuing an unqualified opinion that the financial statements fairly present Amazon’s financial position, results of operations, and cash flows in conformity with U.S. GAAP.
sections.Item 9B: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) states in Item 9B — Other Information of its 2023 Form 10-K that there is no additional information to report. The section is marked “Not applicable,” indicating no other material disclosures, events, or updates beyond the filing’s preceding items.
sections.Item 9A: Amazon.com Inc. reports that, as of December 31, 2022, its disclosure controls and procedures were effective under Rule 13a-15(e) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, providing reasonable assurance that required SEC disclosures are recorded, processed, summarized, and reported on time and communicated to management, including the principal executive officer and principal financial officer. Management also concluded that internal control over financial reporting was effective under the COSO Internal Control—Integrated Framework (2013), with Ernst & Young independently assessing the controls, and stated there were no material changes in internal control during the quarter ended December 31, 2022.
sections.Item 9C: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) states in Item 9C of its 2022 Form 10-K that the disclosure regarding foreign jurisdictions that prevent inspections is not applicable.
