NKE_10K_2025-07-17_full.txt.document_context: NIKE, Inc. filed a 10-K annual report covering fiscal 2025, the year ended May 31, 2025. NIKE said it is the world’s largest seller of athletic footwear and apparel, with products sold through NIKE Direct and wholesale channels in nearly all countries, and that nearly all footwear and apparel are manufactured by independent contractors outside the United States. The company also said it sells under the NIKE, Jordan and Converse brands and is focused on sustainable long-term growth through innovation, consumer connections and digital and retail experiences.

sections.Preamble: NIKE, Inc. filed its Form 10-K annual report for the fiscal year ended May 31, 2025 with the SEC under Commission File No. 1-10635, identifying Oregon as its state of incorporation and listing its principal executive offices at One Bowerman Drive, Beaverton, Oregon. The filing states that NIKE’s Class B Common Stock trades on the New York Stock Exchange under ticker NKE, that the company is a large accelerated filer and well-known seasoned issuer, and that it has timely filed required Exchange Act reports and Interactive Data Files.
sections.Item 1: NIKE, Inc., incorporated in Oregon in 1967, says its core business is the worldwide design, development, and marketing of athletic footwear, apparel, equipment, accessories, and services, making it the largest seller of athletic footwear and apparel globally. The company sells through NIKE Direct retail stores and digital platforms, wholesale accounts, and interactive consumer services, with nearly all products made by independent contractors and most footwear and apparel manufactured outside the United States. NIKE markets products under the NIKE, Jordan, and Converse brands and emphasizes innovation, “must-have” products, consumer connections, and digital and retail experiences to drive sustainable long-term growth.
sections.Item 1B: Nike Inc. (NKE) states in Item 1B of its 2025 Form 10-K that there are no unresolved staff comments. The section indicates that the company had no outstanding comments from the SEC staff requiring further resolution as of the filing date.
sections.Item 1C: Nike says cybersecurity is a core part of its enterprise risk management, with board- and management-level oversight led by Vice President, Corporate Information Security, Risk & Compliance (VP, CIS), who reports to the CIO and ultimately the CTO. The company uses the NIST Cybersecurity Framework, risk assessments, vulnerability management with periodic penetration testing, third-party cyber risk reviews, internal audit testing, and external assessments to monitor and remediate threats, with results reported to the Audit & Finance Committee. Nike also maintains an incident response plan covering detection, escalation, assessment, management, and remediation, and routinely tests the plan across the organization.
sections.Item 3: Nike Inc. states that it does not believe there are any material pending legal proceedings, other than ordinary routine litigation incidental to its business, in which it is a party or whose subject is its property. The company directs readers to Note 16 — Commitments and Contingencies in the accompanying Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements for additional information.
sections.Item 1A: Nike’s Item 1A risk factors describe its market structure and exposure to U.S. and international trade risks, with NIKE Brand operations organized across North America, EMEA, Greater China, and APLA, plus Converse as a separate reportable segment. In fiscal 2025, NIKE Brand and Converse sales in the United States were about 43% of total revenues, the company sold through wholesale accounts and NIKE Direct/Converse digital commerce and stores, and its U.S. footprint included 376 retail stores and eight significant distribution centers. The section also highlights dependence on Sojitz Corporation of America for import-export financing and warns that foreign and domestic trade policy changes, import duties, quotas, sanctions, political instability, and protectionist actions could disrupt sourcing, supply chains, and international operations.
sections.Item 4: Nike Inc. (NKE) states that Item 4, Mine Safety Disclosures, is not applicable in its 2025 Form 10-K filed with the SEC on July 17, 2025. The section contains no mine safety operations, incidents, citations, or related metrics, and simply appears as a brief Part II table-of-contents entry.
sections.Item 2: Nike Inc. reports that non-U.S. NIKE Brand and Converse sales represented about 57% of fiscal 2025 revenues, supported by wholesale accounts, NIKE Direct and Converse digital commerce in over 40 countries, and 72 distribution centers outside the United States. Its international retail footprint includes 658 non-U.S. stores, comprising 543 NIKE Brand factory stores, 61 NIKE Brand in-line stores, and 54 Converse stores, while no customer accounted for 10% or more of consolidated net revenues in fiscal 2025. The section also emphasizes product research, design and development as a core competitive driver, highlighting technical innovation, sustainability, and collaboration with athletes and experts across biomechanics, engineering, chemistry, and related fields.
sections.Item 7A: Nike Inc. says it uses financial instruments only to manage, not trade or speculate, on foreign currency and interest rate exposures arising from international sales, product sourcing, and funding activities. Its foreign exchange program uses forward and option contracts to hedge anticipated and firm commitments, with most derivatives outstanding as of May 31, 2025 designated as foreign currency cash flow hedges for Euro/U.S. Dollar, Chinese Yuan/U.S. Dollar, British Pound/Euro, and Japanese Yen/U.S. Dollar pairs. Nike also manages interest rate risk through a mix of commercial paper, bank loans, fixed-rate debt, and receive-fixed, pay-variable interest rate swaps to limit earnings and cash flow volatility and reduce borrowing costs.
sections.Item 5: NIKE’s Class B Common Stock is listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol NKE, with 20,485 holders of record as of July 9, 2025, while Class A Common Stock is not publicly traded but is convertible into Class B shares. The Board authorized a four-year, $18 billion share repurchase program in June 2022; by May 31, 2025 NIKE had repurchased 122.6 million shares at an average price of $98.00 for about $12.0 billion, including 3.24 million shares bought in the quarter ended May 31, 2025 at an average price of $62.38, leaving about $5.99 billion available. The section also references a five-year performance graph comparing NIKE Class B stock with the S&P 500, the Dow Jones U.S. Footwear Index, and the S&P Apparel, Accessories & Luxury Goods Index.
sections.Item 6: Item 6 of Nike Inc.’s 2025 Form 10-K is reserved and contains no substantive disclosure, financial metrics, or named business developments. The section appears only as a placeholder in the table of contents and on page 28, with no discussion of Nike, NKE, products, operations, or regulatory matters.
sections.Item 9B: Nike Inc. reported that during the fiscal quarter ended May 31, 2025, none of its directors or officers adopted or terminated any Rule 10b5-1 trading arrangement or non-Rule 10b5-1 trading arrangement. The disclosure references Exchange Act Rule 16a-1 and Item 408 of Regulation S-K, and indicates no insider trading plan changes were made in the period.
sections.Item 9A: Nike Inc. states that its disclosure controls and procedures under the Exchange Act are designed to provide reasonable assurance that information required in SEC reports is recorded, processed, summarized, and communicated to management, including the Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer, in time for required disclosure decisions. Based on ongoing evaluation procedures, management concluded these controls were effective at the reasonable assurance level as of May 31, 2025, and Nike reported no material changes in internal control over financial reporting during the most recent fiscal quarter.
sections.Item 9: Nike Inc. states that there has been no change of accountants and no disagreements with its accountants regarding accounting principles, practices, or financial statement disclosure. This Item 9 disclosure in the 2025 Form 10-K reports no matters required to be reported under SEC rules.
sections.Item 7: Nike Inc. says it designs, develops, markets and sells athletic footwear, apparel, equipment, accessories and services worldwide through NIKE Direct (owned stores and digital platforms/NIKE Brand Digital) and wholesale channels, with a strategy focused on sustainable long-term revenue growth, innovation, consumer connections and digital/retail experiences. Fiscal 2025 revenue fell to $46.3 billion from $51.4 billion, NIKE Direct revenue declined 13% to $18.8 billion, wholesale revenue decreased 7% reported, gross margin compressed 190 basis points to 42.7%, inventories were flat at $7.5 billion, and the company returned $5.3 billion to shareholders while ROIC fell to 20.2% from 34.9%. Nike attributes the weaker results to lower traffic in NIKE Direct, increased markdowns, discounts and sales returns tied to reducing supply of certain footwear products, and higher inventory obsolescence reserves, partially offset
sections.Item 8: NIKE, Inc. states that its 2025 Form 10-K financial statements were prepared in accordance with U.S. GAAP using management’s best estimates and judgments, and that its accounting systems include controls, qualified personnel, and segregation of duties to safeguard assets and support reliable reporting. The section also describes oversight by the Audit & Finance Committee of the Board of Directors, which is composed of three outside independent directors and works with the internal corporate audit department and the independent registered public accounting firm on the annual audit, accounting controls, and other financial matters. Management further asserts responsibility for maintaining adequate internal control over financial reporting under Exchange Act Rules 13a-15(f) and 15d-15(f), emphasizing reasonable assurance over the reliability of external financial statements.
sections.Item 11: Nike Inc. states that the executive compensation disclosure required by Items 402, 407(e)(4), and 407(e)(5) of Regulation S-K is not repeated in the Form 10-K but is incorporated by reference from the definitive Proxy Statement for the 2025 Annual Meeting of Shareholders. The referenced proxy materials include “Corporate Governance — Director Compensation for Fiscal 2025,” “Executive Compensation — Compensation Discussion and Analysis,” “Executive Compensation — Executive Compensation Tables,” and “Additional Information — Compensation Committee Interlocks and Insider Participation.”
sections.Item 9C: NIKE, Inc. states in Item 9C that the disclosure regarding foreign jurisdictions that prevent inspections is “Not applicable,” indicating no reportable issues under this SEC requirement. The section also notes that Part III of the 2025 Form 10-K is incorporated by reference from the Proxy Statement for the NIKE, Inc. 2025 Annual Meeting of Shareholders, except for information about executive officers in Item 1.
sections.Item 13: NIKE, Inc. states that the disclosures required by Items 404 and 407(a) of Regulation S-K regarding certain relationships and related transactions and director independence are not repeated in the 10-K, but are incorporated by reference from the definitive Proxy Statement for the 2025 Annual Meeting of Shareholders. The referenced proxy statement sections are “Additional Information — Transactions with Related Persons” and “Corporate Governance — NIKE, Inc. Board of Directors — Director Independence,” covering related-person transactions and board independence for NIKE, Inc. and its directors.
sections.Item 12: Nike Inc. states that the Item 201(d) Regulation S-K disclosure on equity compensation plan information is provided by reference to its definitive Proxy Statement for the 2025 Annual Meeting of Shareholders, under “Executive Compensation — Executive Compensation Tables — Equity Compensation Plan Information.” It also incorporates by reference the Item 403 Regulation S-K disclosure on stock ownership of certain beneficial owners and management from the Proxy Statement’s “Stock Ownership Information — Stock Holdings of Certain Owners and Management” section.
sections.Item 10: NIKE, Inc. states that the Item 401 director disclosures, Item 406 Code of Conduct, Item 407(d)(4) and (d)(5) Audit & Finance Committee information, and Item 408(b)(1) insider trading policies are incorporated by reference from the definitive Proxy Statement for the 2025 Annual Meeting of Shareholders. Executive officer information is provided in Item 1 of the Annual Report under “Information about our Executive Officers,” and the referenced governance materials cover the NIKE, Inc. Board of Directors and Board committees.
sections.Item 14: Nike Inc. states that the disclosure required by Item 9(e) of Schedule 14A regarding principal accountant fees and services is not repeated in the Form 10-K, but is instead incorporated by reference from the definitive Proxy Statement for the 2025 Annual Meeting of Shareholders. The referenced proxy disclosure appears under “Audit Matters — Ratification of Appointment of Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm,” and this Item 14 section is part of Part IV of the 2025 Annual Report.
sections.Item 16: Item 16 contains no substantive business discussion; it includes the “Form 10-K Summary” marked “None,” followed by PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP’s consent to incorporate its July 17, 2025 audit report into NIKE, Inc.’s Form S-3 and multiple Form S-8 registration statements. It also provides the formal signatures for NIKE, Inc.’s 2025 Form 10-K under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, signed on July 17, 2025 by Elliott Hill (President and Chief Executive Officer), Matthew Friend (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer), Johanna Nielsen (Vice President, Chief Accounting Officer), and directors including Mark Parker, Timothy Cook, Thasunda Duckett, Mónica Gil, Maria Henry, Peter Henry, Travis Knight, Michelle Peluso, John Rogers, Jr., and Robert Swan.
sections.Item 15: Item 15 lists NIKE, Inc.’s filed annual report materials, including the PCAOB ID238 independent registered public accounting firm report and the core consolidated financial statements for fiscal years ended May 31, 2025, 2024, and 2023: income, comprehensive income, balance sheets, cash flows, shareholders’ equity, and notes. NVDA_10K_2022Q1_2022-03-18_full.txt.document_context: NVIDIA Corporation filed a 10-K annual report for the fiscal year ended 2022-01-30. The excerpt says NVIDIA has invested over $29 billion in research and development since inception and highlights its platform strategy built around GPUs and software stacks for gaming, AI, data science, autonomous vehicles, robotics, and AR/VR.

sections.Preamble: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) filed this Form 10-K annual report with the SEC for the fiscal year ended January 30, 2022, under Commission file number 0-23985. The filing identifies NVIDIA as a Delaware corporation headquartered at 2788 San Tomas Expressway, Santa Clara, California 95051, with common stock listed on The Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol NVDA and no securities registered under Section 12(g). It also states that NVIDIA is a well-known seasoned issuer, has filed all required Exchange Act reports and Interactive Data Files for the preceding 12 months, and qualifies as a large accelerated filer.
sections.Item 1: NVIDIA Corporation describes itself as a pioneer of accelerated computing, expanding from PC graphics into GPU-based platforms for gaming, scientific computing, artificial intelligence, data science, autonomous vehicles, robotics, and augmented/virtual reality. The company emphasizes its platform strategy built on a unified GPU architecture, software stacks, algorithms, libraries, and services, with deep learning adoption by thousands of enterprises and support for multi-billion-dollar end markets through internal and third-party developer ecosystems. NVIDIA also highlights more than $29 billion in cumulative R&D since inception, its 1999 invention of the GPU, and the 2006 CUDA programming model that opened GPU parallel processing for general-purpose computing and high-performance computing applications.
sections.Item 2: NVIDIA Corporation states that its headquarters is in Santa Clara, California, where it owns and leases approximately 1.76 million square feet of office and building space, including a new campus building completed in February 2022. The company also leases data center space in Santa Clara and owns or leases facilities for data centers, research and development, sales, and administrative functions across the U.S. and internationally, primarily in Asia, Israel, and Europe. NVIDIA says its owned and leased facilities are in good condition and suitable for its business, and it does not identify or allocate assets by operating segment.
sections.Item 1B: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) states that Item 1B, Unresolved Staff Comments, is not applicable in its 2022 Form 10-K filed with the SEC on 2022-03-18. The section indicates there were no unresolved comments from the Securities and Exchange Commission staff requiring disclosure.
sections.Item 1A: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 1A risk factors section highlights ESG and climate-change-related risks and governance, noting oversight by the Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee and annual ESG reviews aligned to stakeholder expectations, market trends, and business risks. The company says its CSR Report covers environmental metrics, that fiscal 2022 metrics were expected in May 2022, and that global sustainability regulations and renewable-energy sourcing had not materially affected capital expenditures, results of operations, or competitive position; NVIDIA also set a goal to match 100% of global electricity usage with renewable energy by fiscal 2025. The section emphasizes NVIDIA’s energy-efficient GPU and AI computing products, including claims that GPU servers are about 40x more energy efficient than traditional CPU servers for AI workloads, that it powered 23 of the top 25 systems on the November 2021 Green500 list, and that it plans to build Earth-2, an AI supercomputer to model climate change impacts.
sections.Item 3: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) states that its legal proceedings are discussed in Note 13 of the Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements in Part IV, Item 15 of this Annual Report on Form 10-K. This Item 3 section contains no substantive litigation details itself and instead cross-references the consolidated financial statement note for information on NVIDIA’s legal matters.
sections.Item 4: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) states that Item 4, Mine Safety Disclosures, is not applicable in its 2022 Form 10-K annual report filed with the SEC on March 18, 2022. The section contains no mine safety incidents, citations, or related metrics, reflecting that NVIDIA is not engaged in mining operations subject to Mine Safety and Health Administration reporting.
sections.Item 5: NVIDIA Corporation’s common stock trades on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol NVDA, with public trading beginning January 22, 1999, and about 313 registered shareholders as of March 11, 2022. The company completed a four-for-one stock split on July 19, 2021, retroactively adjusting all share and per-share amounts, and reported paying $399 million in quarterly cash dividends during fiscal 2022. NVIDIA also disclosed a long-running share repurchase program authorized by its Board of Directors since August 2004, with 1.04 billion shares repurchased for $7.08 billion through January 30, 2022, no repurchases in fiscal 2022, and authorization to repurchase up to $7.24 billion through December 2022 under Rule 10b-18.
sections.Item 7: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 7 Management’s Discussion and Analysis in its 2022 Form 10-K provides management’s narrative on financial condition and results of operations, intended to be read alongside the company’s consolidated financial statements and related notes. The section frames the discussion around NVIDIA’s business performance, liquidity, and operating trends for the fiscal year ended 2022-01-30, with emphasis on factors affecting revenue, expenses, and overall financial condition.
sections.Item 9: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) reported no changes in accountants and no disagreements with accountants on accounting or financial disclosure for the 2022 Form 10-K filed with the SEC on March 18, 2022. This Item 9 section states “None,” indicating no issues to disclose regarding the company’s independent auditors or financial reporting practices.
sections.Item 8: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 8 states that the required financial statements and supplementary data are provided in the company’s Consolidated Financial Statements and Notes included in this Annual Report on Form 10-K. This section serves as a cross-reference to the audited financial reporting package for NVIDIA’s fiscal year ended in the 2022 annual filing.
sections.Item 9B: Item 9B, Other Information, states that NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) had no additional information to report in this section of its 2022 Form 10-K annual report filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on March 18, 2022. The section is effectively empty, indicating “None.”
sections.Item 7A: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) says its market risk exposure is primarily to interest rates and foreign exchange, with a fixed-rate investment portfolio and $11.00 billion of senior notes outstanding as of January 30, 2022. A sensitivity analysis found that a parallel 0.5% shift in the yield curve would change investment fair value by $33 million down or $22 million up, while the fixed-rate Notes create no financial statement interest-rate risk. Foreign exchange risk is described as minimal, with U.S.-dollar pricing for third-party manufacturing, some international operating expenses, and the use of foreign currency forward contracts designated as cash flow hedges to offset currency movements.
sections.Item 9C: NVIDIA Corporation states that Item 9C, “Disclosure Regarding Foreign Jurisdictions That Prevent Inspections,” is not applicable in this Form 10-K. The company also notes that certain Part III information is omitted from the annual report because it will be provided in its definitive proxy statement filed with the SEC under Regulation 14A, the 2022 Proxy Statement, within 120 days after the end of fiscal year 2022.
sections.Item 10: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 10 in its 2022 Form 10-K largely incorporates by reference governance disclosures from the 2022 Proxy Statement, including director nominations, executive officers, the Audit Committee and financial experts, procedures for recommending directors, and delinquent Section 16(a) reports under the Exchange Act. It also points investors to NVIDIA’s Code of Conduct and Financial Team Code of Conduct on the Investor Relations section of www.nvidia.com, while stating that website contents are not part of the Annual Report.
sections.Item 11: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 11 states that the required executive compensation disclosure is not included in the 10-K itself but will be provided in the company’s 2022 Proxy Statement. The incorporated proxy sections include “Executive Compensation,” “Compensation Committee Interlocks and Insider Participation,” “Director Compensation,” and “Compensation Committee Report,” making the proxy statement the key source for compensation and governance details.
sections.Item 9A: NVIDIA Corporation’s management, including the Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer, concluded as of January 30, 2022 that its disclosure controls and procedures and internal control over financial reporting were effective, based on the COSO Internal Control—Integrated Framework (2013) and Rule 13a-15 under the Exchange Act. PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP audited the effectiveness of internal control over financial reporting, and NVIDIA reported no material changes during the quarter ended January 30, 2022, while noting an ongoing phased upgrade of its enterprise resource planning (ERP) system, including a consolidated financial reporting and general ledger module planned for fiscal 2023.
sections.Item 14: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) states that the information required by Item 14 on principal accountant fees and services is not presented in the 2022 Form 10-K itself but is incorporated by reference from the 2022 Proxy Statement. The referenced proxy disclosure appears under the caption “Fees Billed by the Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm,” and the section is located in Part IV of the annual report.
sections.Item 12: Item 12 of NVIDIA Corporation’s 2022 Form 10-K states that required information on security ownership of NVIDIA securities will be provided in the company’s 2022 Proxy Statement under “Security Ownership of Certain Beneficial Owners and Management” and incorporated by reference. It also says details on NVIDIA’s equity compensation plans will appear in the 2022 Proxy Statement under “Equity Compensation Plan Information,” likewise incorporated by reference.
sections.Item 13: NVIDIA Corporation states that the information required by Item 13 on certain relationships and related transactions, as well as director independence, is not presented in the 2022 Form 10-K itself but will be included in the 2022 Proxy Statement. The filing incorporates by reference the proxy statement sections titled “Review of Transactions with Related Persons” and “Information About the Board of Directors and Corporate Governance - Independence of the Members of the Board of Directors.”
sections.Item 15: Item 15 lists NVIDIA Corporation’s audited financial statements and schedules for fiscal years ended January 30, 2022, January 31, 2021, and January 26, 2020, including consolidated income, comprehensive income, balance sheets, shareholders’ equity, cash flows, notes, and Schedule II valuation and qualifying accounts. It also identifies the independent registered public accounting firm’s report (PCAOB ID: 238), which states that NVIDIA’s consolidated financial statements and internal control over financial reporting were audited under COSO 2013 criteria and presented fairly in conformity with U.S. GAAP.
sections.Item 16: Item 16 contains NVIDIA Corporation’s 10-K signatures and power-of-attorney language, confirming the report was duly authorized and filed under Section 13 or 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 on March 17, 2022. It names Jen-Hsun Huang (President, Chief Executive Officer and Director), Colette M. Kress (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer), Donald Robertson (Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer), and the board of directors, and authorizes Huang and Kress to sign amendments and related SEC filings.

sections.Item 15: Item 15 of NVIDIA Corporation’s 2024 Form 10-K lists the audited financial statements, financial statement schedule, and exhibits included in the annual report, with page references for the consolidated income, comprehensive income, balance sheet, shareholders’ equity, cash flow statements, and Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements. NVDA_10K_2025-02-26_full.txt.document_context: NVIDIA Corporation filed a 10-K annual report for the fiscal year ended January 26, 2025. The company describes itself as a full-stack computing infrastructure provider, with CUDA, hundreds of software libraries/SDKs/APIs, and data-center-scale compute and networking designed to support AI model training and inference across tens of thousands of GPU-accelerated servers.

sections.Item 1B: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) states in Item 1B, Unresolved Staff Comments, that there were no unresolved comments from the SEC staff as of the filing date of its 2025 Form 10-K. The section is marked “Not applicable,” indicating no outstanding regulatory correspondence or comment-resolution issues to disclose.
sections.Preamble: NVIDIA Corporation’s Form 10-K annual report for the fiscal year ended January 26, 2025 identifies the company as a Delaware corporation headquartered at 2788 San Tomas Expressway, Santa Clara, California 95051, with telephone number (408) 486-2000 and IRS Employer Identification No. 94-3177549. The filing states that NVIDIA’s common stock ($0.001 par value) trades on The Nasdaq Global Select Market under ticker NVDA, that there are no securities registered under Section 12(g), and that the company is a large accelerated filer that has filed all required Exchange Act reports and Interactive Data Files for the preceding 12 months.
sections.Item 2: NVIDIA Corporation states that its headquarters are in Santa Clara, California, where it owns and leases approximately 3 million square feet of office and building space and also leases data center space. The company further owns and leases facilities for data centers, research and development, and sales and administrative functions across the U.S. and internationally, primarily in China, India, Israel, and Taiwan, and says these facilities are in good condition and suitable for its business. NVIDIA does not identify or allocate assets by operating segment and refers readers to Note 17 of the Consolidated Financial Statements for lease obligations.
sections.Item 1A: NVIDIA Corporation says compliance with existing and future laws and regulations — including IP ownership and infringement, taxes, import/export controls and tariffs, anti-corruption, foreign exchange and cash repatriation restrictions, data privacy, competition and antitrust, cybersecurity, environmental, health and safety, responsible AI, climate change, cryptocurrency, and consumer laws — could raise costs and materially harm its business, financial condition, and results of operations. The company also highlights sustainability and governance oversight by the Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee, annual assessment of ESG programs, and climate-related risk management across the product lifecycle, noting that May 2024 environmental impact metrics were published for fiscal 2024 and fiscal 2025 metrics are expected in the first half of fiscal 2026.
sections.Item 3: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) states that its legal proceedings are discussed in Note 12 of the Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements in Part IV, Item 15 of this Annual Report on Form 10-K. This Item 3 section contains no additional substantive details about specific lawsuits, regulators, claims, or financial exposure beyond directing readers to the referenced note.
sections.Item 1C: NVIDIA Corporation says its cybersecurity risk management and strategy are built around infrastructure, systems, policies, and procedures to proactively and reactively address incidents, with risk identification and response integrated into its broader enterprise risk management program. The company’s information security management program generally follows ISO 27001, and NVIDIA consults external cybersecurity firms and governance experts while maintaining cybersecurity and data privacy training, policies, and vendor risk assessments for suppliers handling confidential data. NVIDIA also routinely evaluates high-risk suppliers against standards such as ISO 27001, ISO 28001, and C-TPAT, and imposes additional information, product, and physical security requirements to mitigate third-party risk.
sections.Item 4: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) states that Item 4, Mine Safety Disclosures, is not applicable in its 2025 Form 10-K annual report filed with the SEC on February 26, 2025. The section contains no mine safety metrics, incidents, citations, or regulatory disclosures because NVIDIA does not report operations subject to mine safety requirements.
sections.Item 7: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 7 Management’s Discussion and Analysis in this 2025 Form 10-K introduces the company’s discussion of financial condition and results of operations, to be read together with the accompanying financial statements and notes. As the opening of MD&A, it frames the section that will explain NVIDIA’s performance, liquidity, and operating trends for the fiscal year ended 2025-02-26.
sections.Item 8: Item 8 of NVIDIA Corporation’s 2025 Form 10-K states that the required financial statements and supplementary data are provided in the company’s Consolidated Financial Statements and Notes included in the Annual Report. This section serves as the gateway to NVIDIA’s audited financial reporting, with no additional narrative beyond directing readers to the consolidated statements and related notes.
sections.Item 6: Item 6 of NVIDIA Corporation’s 2025 Form 10-K is reserved and contains no substantive disclosure, consisting only of a placeholder reference in the table of contents. This section does not provide any financial, operational, regulatory, product, or risk information beyond the “[Reserved]” notation.
sections.Item 7A: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) says its market risk exposure is primarily to interest rates on its fixed-rate investment portfolio and $8.5 billion of senior notes outstanding, with a fiscal 2025 sensitivity analysis showing a plus or minus 0.5% parallel shift in the yield curve would change investment fair value by $238 million. The company also describes minimal foreign exchange risk because substantially all sales and third-party manufacturing arrangements are denominated in U.S. dollars, while foreign currency forward contracts hedge exposures; a 10% stronger U.S. dollar would have reduced accumulated other comprehensive income by $136 million and $116 million before tax as of January 26, 2025 and January 28, 2024, respectively.
sections.Item 5:
NVIDIA Corporation’s common stock trades on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol NVDA, with public trading beginning January 22, 1999; as of February 21, 2025, the company had approximately 842 registered shareholders. In May and June 2024, NVIDIA completed a ten-for-one stock split by amending its Restated Certificate of Incorporation with the Delaware Secretary of State, increasing authorized common stock from 8.0 billion to 80.0 billion shares and retrospectively adjusting all per-share amounts.  

The section also highlights capital return activity: on August 26, 2024, the Board authorized an additional $50 billion for share repurchases without expiration, and in fiscal 2025 NVIDIA repurchased 310 million shares for $34.0 billion and paid $834 million in cash dividends. As of January 26, 2025, $38.7 billion remained available under the repurchase program, with buybacks permitted
sections.Item 9: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) reports no changes in accountants and no disagreements with accountants on accounting or financial disclosure in Item 9 of its 2025 Form 10-K. The section states “None,” indicating no issues to disclose with respect to the company’s independent auditors or related financial reporting matters.
sections.Item 9C: NVIDIA Corporation states that Item 9C, “Disclosure Regarding Foreign Jurisdictions that Prevent Inspections,” is not applicable in this Form 10-K. The company also notes that certain Part III information is omitted from the annual report because it will be provided in its definitive 2025 Proxy Statement filed with the SEC under Regulation 14A within 120 days after fiscal 2025 year-end, and incorporated by reference into the report.
sections.Item 10: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 10 cross-references its 2025 Proxy Statement for information on directors, executive officers, the Audit Committee, board governance, and procedures for recommending directors, rather than repeating those details in the Form 10-K. It also points investors to the company’s Code of Conduct and Financial Team Code of Conduct on NVIDIA’s Investor Relations website and states that any amendments or waivers for executive officers or directors will be disclosed on the website or in a Form 8-K, while the insider trading policy is incorporated by reference under Item 408(b) of Regulation S-K.
sections.Item 11: NVIDIA Corporation states that the executive compensation information required by Item 11 is not included in the 10-K itself but will be provided in its 2025 Proxy Statement. The filing incorporates by reference the Proxy Statement sections titled “Executive Compensation,” “Compensation Committee Interlocks and Insider Participation,” “Director Compensation,” and “Compensation Committee Report.”
sections.Item 12: Item 12 of NVIDIA Corporation’s 2025 Form 10-K states that required disclosure on security ownership of certain beneficial owners and management will be provided in the 2025 Proxy Statement under “Security Ownership of Certain Beneficial Owners and Management,” and is incorporated by reference. It also states that information on NVIDIA’s equity compensation plans will appear in the 2025 Proxy Statement under “Equity Compensation Plan Information,” likewise incorporated by reference.
sections.Item 9A: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) reported that, as of January 26, 2025, its disclosure controls and procedures were effective under Exchange Act Rule 13a-15(e) and provided reasonable assurance that required Exchange Act information is recorded, processed, summarized, reported, and communicated to management, including the Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer, in time for timely disclosure decisions. Management also concluded that internal control over financial reporting was effective under the COSO Internal Control—Integrated Framework (2013), and this assessment was audited by PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP; NVIDIA stated there were no material changes in internal control during the quarter, while noting its ongoing phased ERP system upgrade begun in fiscal 2022 to update core financial systems.
sections.Item 13: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) states that the Item 13 disclosure on certain relationships and related transactions, as well as director independence, is not included in the 10-K text itself but will be provided in the company’s 2025 Proxy Statement. The referenced proxy statement sections are “Review of Transactions with Related Persons” and “Information About the Board of Directors and Corporate Governance — Independence of the Members of the Board of Directors,” which are incorporated by reference.
sections.Item 14: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) states that the information required by Item 14 regarding principal accountant fees and services is not presented in the 10-K itself but will be included in the 2025 Proxy Statement under the caption “Fees Billed by the Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm.” The filing incorporates that proxy disclosure by reference, indicating that details on fees paid to the independent registered public accounting firm are deferred to the proxy materials.
sections.Item 16: Item 16 contains NVIDIA Corporation’s 10-K signatures and power-of-attorney language, with the report signed on February 26, 2025 by President and Chief Executive Officer Jen-Hsun Huang. It states that the registrant is filing under Section 13 or 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and appoints Jen-Hsun Huang and Colette M. Kress as attorneys-in-fact to sign amendments and related filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
sections.Item 15: Item 15 lists NVIDIA Corporation’s audited financial statements and schedules for the fiscal years ended January 26, 2025, January 28, 2024, and January 29, 2023, including the consolidated income, comprehensive income, balance sheet, shareholders’ equity, and cash flow statements, plus Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements and Schedule II Valuation and Qualifying Accounts. It also identifies the Report of Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm (PCAOB ID: 238), which states that the consolidated financial statements and NVIDIA’s internal control over financial reporting were audited under COSO Internal Control—Integrated Framework (2013) and presented fairly in conformity with U.S. GAAP.
sections.Item 1: NVIDIA Corporation describes itself as a full-stack accelerated computing infrastructure company built around the CUDA programming model, GPUs, software libraries, SDKs, APIs, and networking systems that scale data centers to tens of thousands of GPU-accelerated servers. The section emphasizes NVIDIA’s role in AI model training and inference, data analytics, scientific computing, and 3D graphics, with vertical-specific solutions for healthcare, telecom, automotive, and manufacturing. It also highlights GPU-powered generative AI and recommendation systems, positioning NVIDIA’s platform strategy as a unified hardware-software architecture for modern AI applications.

NVDA_10Q_2022Q2_2022-05-27_full.txt.document_context: NVIDIA Corporation filed a 10-Q quarterly report for the fiscal quarter ended May 1, 2022. The excerpt shows revenue of $8.288 billion, up from $5.661 billion a year earlier, with gross profit of $5.431 billion and operating expenses including $1.618 billion in research and development and $592 million in sales, general and administrative.

sections.Preamble: NVIDIA Corporation’s Form 10-Q for the quarterly period ended May 1, 2022 was filed with the SEC under Commission file number 0-23985, identifying the company as a Delaware corporation headquartered at 2788 San Tomas Expressway, Santa Clara, California 95051, with common stock trading on The Nasdaq Global Select Market under ticker NVDA. sections.Item 3: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) states that, as of May 1, 2022, there were no material changes to its financial market risks since the fiscal year ended January 30, 2022, including no material changes from investment and interest rate risk or foreign exchange rate risk, despite the COVID-19 pandemic. The section cross-references the company’s Annual Report on Form 10-K and Note 11 of the Notes to Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements for additional detail.
sections.Item 1: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 1 section points readers to its investor relations website, SEC filings, press releases, conference calls/webcasts, and social media channels (Twitter, blog, Facebook, LinkedIn, Instagram, YouTube) for material disclosures under Regulation FD. The unaudited condensed consolidated financial statements for the quarter ended May 1, 2022 show revenue of $8.288 billion versus $5.661 billion a year earlier, gross profit of $5.431 billion, cost of revenue of $2.857 billion, and operating expenses including research and development of $1.618 billion and sales, general and administrative of $592 million.
sections.Item 4: NVIDIA Corporation’s management, including the Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer, concluded as of May 1, 2022 that disclosure controls and procedures were effective under Rule 13a-15(e) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, providing reasonable assurance. The company reported no material changes in internal control over financial reporting during the first quarter of fiscal 2023, while noting an ongoing phased ERP system upgrade begun in fiscal 2022 that will update core financial systems, including the consolidated financial reporting and general ledger module in fiscal 2023. NVIDIA also reiterated the inherent limitations of internal controls, stating they cannot prevent all error or fraud and provide only reasonable, not absolute, assurance.
sections.Item 1A: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 1A risk factors say there have been no material changes from the prior 10-K, but warn that investing in NVDA common stock involves risks that could hurt business, financial condition, results of operations, and stock price. The section emphasizes demand-forecasting risk tied to product introductions and transitions, channel partners, competitor releases, macroeconomic conditions including rising inflation, and the company’s GPU exposure to cryptocurrency mining demand, especially Ethereum and the pending Ethereum 2.0 standard, amid volatile crypto prices, government policies, and blockchain verification changes.
sections.Item 2: NVIDIA Corporation’s 10-Q includes extensive forward-looking statement disclaimers, warning that actual results may differ materially due to known and unknown risks and referring readers to the Risk Factors section of this filing and the company’s fiscal 2022 Form 10-K. It also identifies NVIDIA and its subsidiaries as the “Company” and lists key trademarks and products including GeForce, GeForce NOW, Mellanox, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, NVIDIA DGX, NVIDIA DRIVE Orin, NVIDIA Grace, NVIDIA Hopper, NVIDIA Omniverse, NVIDIA OVX, NVIDIA RTX, NVIDIA Spectrum, Quadro, and MAXQ®, with features, pricing, availability, and specifications subject to change without notice.
sections.Item 6: Item 6 lists NVIDIA Corporation’s exhibits for the Form 10-Q, including the Fiscal Year 2023 Variable Compensation Plan (Exhibit 10.1), CEO and CFO certifications under Rule 13a-14(a) and 13a-14(b) (Exhibits 31.1, 31.2, 32.1, 32.2), and Inline XBRL materials such as the instance, taxonomy schema, calculation, definition, labels, presentation linkbases, and cover page interactive data file (Exhibits 101 and 104). The section also notes SEC Regulation S-K Item 601(b)(32)(ii) treatment of the Section 32 certifications as furnished rather than filed, and provides NVIDIA Investor Relations in Santa Clara, California as the contact for copies of exhibits.

sections.Item 1A: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) says there have been no material changes to its Item 1A risk factors since its January 30, 2022 Form 10-K and May 1, 2022 Form 10-Q, but warns that investing in its common stock involves risks that could hurt business, financial condition, results of operations, and stock price. The section emphasizes dependence on third-party semiconductor manufacturers and assemblers, long manufacturing lead times, uncertain customer demand, and limited wafer/component/capacity supply, which can create mismatches between supply and demand, product shortages, excess inventory, and revenue and cash flow volatility. NVIDIA also notes that industry shortages have pushed certain supply lead times beyond twelve months from a historical six months, forcing it to pay premiums and deposits to secure future supply and capacity, increasing product costs and potentially leaving it with inflexible supply commitments if revenue declines.

The results were driven by surging Data Center demand for accelerated computing, large language models, generative and agentic AI, and the ramp of Blackwell and Blackwell Ultra, while Networking grew to $7.3 billion on NVLink, XDR InfiniBand, and Ethernet AI adoption; NVIDIA also disclosed a $180 million release of reserved H20 inventory tied to about $650 million of H20 sales to an unrestricted customer outside China and no H20 sales to China-based customers.
sections.Item 3: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 3 discusses market risk rather than legal proceedings, focusing on investment, interest rate, and foreign exchange exposure. As of July 27, 2025, a hypothetical 10% decline in publicly-held equity securities would reduce fair value by $320 million, while non-marketable equity securities in privately held companies are valued at cost minus impairment and adjusted for observable price changes; NVIDIA also states there have been no material changes in foreign exchange rate risk since January 26, 2025.
sections.Item 5: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 5 discloses a Rule 10b5-1 trading arrangement involving Board Director A. Brooke Seawell, who terminated a plan on July 14, 2025 that had been adopted on March 19, 2025 and was intended to cover sales through July 31, 2025. The arrangement contemplated up to 1,153,049 shares of common stock, and 770,522 shares were actually sold before termination, highlighting insider stock-sale activity under Rule 10b5-1(c).
sections.Item 4: NVIDIA Corporation reported that, as of July 27, 2025, management including the Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer concluded its disclosure controls and procedures were effective under Exchange Act Rule 13a-15(e) to ensure required SEC information is recorded, processed, summarized, reported, and communicated on time. The company also stated there were no changes in internal control over financial reporting during the second quarter of fiscal 2026 that materially affected controls, while noting an ongoing phased upgrade of its enterprise resource planning (ERP) system to update core financial systems. NVIDIA further emphasized the inherent limitations of any control system, including that controls can provide only reasonable, not absolute, assurance against error and fraud.

NVDA_10Q_2025Q4_2025-11-19_full.txt.document_context: NVIDIA Corporation filed a 10-Q quarterly report for the fiscal quarter ended October 26, 2025, with unaudited condensed consolidated financial statements for the three and nine months ended October 26, 2025 and October 27, 2024. The excerpt shows the report includes balance sheets as of October 26, 2025 and January 26, 2025, cash flow statements for the nine-month periods, and notes to the financial statements.

sections.Preamble: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) filed a Form 10-Q quarterly report under Sections 13/15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 for the quarter ended October 26, 2025, with Commission File Number 0-23985 and principal executive offices at 2788 San Tomas Expressway, Santa Clara, California 95051. The filing identifies NVIDIA as a Delaware corporation, a large accelerated filer and non-shell company, with common stock listed on The Nasdaq Global Select Market and 24.3 billion shares outstanding as of November 14, 2025.
sections.Item 1: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 1 in this 10-Q is the Financial Information section, which presents the unaudited condensed consolidated financial statements for the three and nine months ended October 26, 2025 and October 27, 2024, including income, comprehensive income, balance sheets, shareholders’ equity, cash flows, and notes. It also highlights NVIDIA’s investor relations and disclosure channels—its investor relations website, SEC filings, press releases, conference calls/webcasts, and social media accounts such as the NVIDIA Corporate Blog, Technical Blog, LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, X, Threads, and YouTube—used to disseminate material information under Regulation FD.
sections.Item 2: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 2 discussion is largely a forward-looking statements disclaimer under the Securities Act of 1933 and Securities Exchange Act of 1934, emphasizing that terms like “may,” “will,” “expect,” and “anticipate” identify estimates and assumptions subject to known and unknown risks and uncertainties. The company cautions that actual results, performance, time frames, and achievements may differ materially, refers readers to the Risk Factors in its fiscal 2025 Form 10-K and this Form 10-Q, and states it has no obligation to update these statements except as required by law.
sections.Item 4: NVIDIA Corporation reported that, as of October 26, 2025, management including the Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer concluded its disclosure controls and procedures were effective under Exchange Act Rule 13a-15(e) to provide reasonable assurance that required information is recorded, processed, summarized, and reported on time and communicated for timely disclosure decisions. The company also stated there were no material changes in internal control over financial reporting during the third quarter of fiscal 2026, while noting an ongoing phased upgrade of its enterprise resource planning (ERP) system to update core financial systems. NVIDIA further emphasized the inherent limitations of any control system, including that controls can provide only reasonable, not absolute, assurance against error and fraud.
sections.Item 3: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 3 disclosure focuses on market risk rather than litigation, describing investment and interest rate risk tied to its marketable equity securities and non-marketable equity securities, as well as foreign exchange rate risk. NVIDIA states that a hypothetical 10% decline in publicly held equity securities would reduce fair value by $387 million as of October 26, 2025, and notes that non-marketable equity valuations are complex because they rely on cost, impairment, and observable price changes in privately held companies. The company also says there have been no material changes in foreign currency transaction risk since January 26, 2025, referencing Part II, Item 7A of its 2024 Form 10-K and Notes 5 and 6 to the condensed consolidated financial statements.
sections.Item 1A: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 1A risk-factor section is preceded by a third-quarter fiscal 2026 operating summary showing revenue of $57.0 billion, up 62% year over year and 22% sequentially, with gross margin of 73.4%, operating income of $36.0 billion, net income of $31.9 billion, and diluted EPS of $1.30. The results were driven primarily by Data Center revenue of $51.2 billion, including $43.0 billion of compute and $8.2 billion of networking, with growth tied to accelerated computing, powerful AI models, agentic applications, Blackwell Ultra and prior Blackwell demand, NVLink compute fabric for GB200/GB300 systems, XDR InfiniBand, and Ethernet for AI solutions; Gaming, Professional Visualization, and Automotive also grew, while H20 sales were insignificant. Gross margin declined year over year as NVIDIA shifted from Hopper HGX systems to Blackwell
sections.Item 6: Item 6 lists NVIDIA Corporation’s filed exhibits for the Form 10-Q, including CEO and CFO certifications under Exchange Act Rule 13a-14(a) and 13a-14(b), Inline XBRL instance, schema, calculation, definition, labels, and presentation linkbase documents, plus the cover page interactive data file. It also notes that Exhibits 32.1 and 32.2 are furnished under Item 601(b)(32)(ii) of Regulation S-K and SEC Release Nos. 33-8238 and 34-47986, are not deemed “filed” under Section 18 of the Exchange Act, and that copies may be requested from NVIDIA Investor Relations in Santa Clara, California.
sections.Item 5: NVIDIA Corporation’s Item 5 discloses Rule 10b5-1 trading arrangements adopted by certain directors and officers intended to satisfy the affirmative defense conditions of Rule 10b5-1(c). The named insiders are Debora Shoquist, Executive Vice President, Operations, who adopted a plan on 9/9/2025 for 309,840 shares expiring 12/31/2026; Donald Robertson, Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer, who adopted a plan on 9/18/2025 for 138,042 shares (including 38,042 future vesting shares, net of tax withholding) expiring 12/31/2026; and Ajay Puri, Executive Vice President, Worldwide Field Operations, who adopted a plan on 9/19/2025 for 1,000,000 shares expiring 6/24/2026.

ORCL_10K_2025-06-18_full.txt.document_context: Oracle Corporation’s 10-K annual report covers the fiscal year ended May 31, 2025. The excerpt says Oracle’s strategy is to offer flexible, interoperable deployment models across on-premise, cloud-based, and hybrid environments, and highlights Oracle Cloud Services (OCA and OCI) as integrated cloud applications and infrastructure delivered and managed by Oracle. It also notes that substantially all customers buy license support contracts with Oracle licenses, and that customers generally purchase hardware support contracts with Oracle hardware products.

sections.Item 1B: Oracle Corporation (ORCL) states that there were no unresolved staff comments in Item 1B of its 2025 Form 10-K annual report filed with the SEC. The section indicates that the company had no outstanding comments from the Securities and Exchange Commission staff requiring further resolution.
sections.Item 1A: Oracle Corporation’s Item 1A risk factors emphasize that its business operates in rapidly changing economic and technological environments, with intense competition, fast product cycles, evolving communications standards, and shifting delivery models affecting its license, hardware, and cloud offerings. Oracle warns that failure to develop differentiated products, integrate acquired offerings, meet service-level commitments, manage data center capacity and infrastructure costs, or sustain renewals of license support, hardware support, and cloud subscription contracts could materially hurt future success. The section also highlights exposure to supply chain and energy costs, sanctions, tariffs, export controls, geopolitical instability, and other regulatory or trade barriers that could disrupt demand and operations.
sections.Item 1: Oracle Corporation’s Item 1 describes a global enterprise IT business centered on flexible, interoperable deployment models spanning on-premise, cloud-based, and hybrid environments, with a strategy focused on customer choice versus competitors that offer fewer options. Its core offerings include Oracle Cloud Applications (OCA) and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), collectively Oracle Cloud Services, along with cloud and on-premise license products such as Oracle Applications, Oracle Database, and Oracle Middleware, plus Oracle hardware including Engineered Systems, servers, and storage. The section also notes that most license and hardware customers buy support contracts, and that Oracle provides professional services to help customers deploy and manage these technologies.
sections.Preamble: Oracle Corporation’s 2025 Form 10-K is an annual report for the fiscal year ended May 31, 2025, filed under Commission File Number 001-35992 with the SEC pursuant to Sections 13 or 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. The filing identifies Oracle as a Delaware corporation headquartered at 2300 Oracle Way, Austin, Texas 78741, with common stock and 3.125% senior notes due July 2025 listed on the New York Stock Exchange under ticker ORCL, and states that Oracle is a well-known seasoned issuer and a large accelerated filer.
sections.Item 2: Oracle Corporation’s Item 2 — Properties describes a global real estate footprint supporting cloud operations, sales, support, research and development, services, manufacturing, and administrative functions. Oracle owns its 0.9 million-square-foot headquarters in Austin, Texas, owns or leases about 26.9 million square feet of additional facilities worldwide, and notes that 10.4 million square feet, or 37% of its total owned and leased space, is sublet or being marketed for sublease or disposition. The company also leases its principal internal manufacturing facility in Hillsboro, Oregon, and operates Oracle Cloud Services through global data centers, substantially all leased via colocation suppliers.
sections.Item 1C: Oracle Corporation says its information security risk management program is designed to assess, identify, prioritize, and manage major cybersecurity exposures, using risk assessments and industry-standard security frameworks to evaluate controls and guide oversight. The company states that Oracle, its vendors, and its cloud/software offerings are frequent targets for hackers because they store and process sensitive data, and that a large-scale cyberattack or security incident could cause unauthorized access, data exfiltration, service disruption, significant expenses, and reputational harm. Oracle also disclosed that it experienced cybersecurity incidents during fiscal 2025, but said they had not had a material impact on its business, results of operations, or financial condition.
sections.Item 3: Oracle Corporation’s Item 3, Legal Proceedings, incorporates by reference the disclosures in Note 12 and Note 15 of the Consolidated Financial Statements, which cover contingencies related to income taxes and legal contingencies. The section indicates that Oracle’s material legal proceedings and related risks are addressed through these notes in Item 15 of the 2025 Form 10-K, rather than described in detail here.
sections.Item 4: Oracle Corporation (ORCL) states that Item 4, Mine Safety Disclosures, is not applicable in its 2025 Form 10-K annual report filed with the SEC on June 18, 2025. The section contains no mine safety metrics, incidents, citations, or regulatory disclosures, indicating Oracle has no relevant mining operations or related reporting obligations under this item.
sections.Item 6: Item 6 of Oracle Corporation’s 2025 Form 10-K is reserved and contains no substantive disclosure. The section consists only of the “[Reserved]” notation and table of contents pagination, with no additional business, financial, regulatory, or product information.
sections.Item 8: Oracle Corporation (NYSE: ORCL) states that the response to Item 8, Financial Statements and Supplementary Data, is provided in a separate section of the Annual Report. The filing directs readers to Part IV, Item 15 for the company’s financial statements and related supplementary data.
sections.Item 7: Oracle Corporation’s Item 7 MD&A outlines its enterprise IT business, including enterprise applications and infrastructure delivered globally through on-premise, cloud-based, and hybrid deployment models, sold via a worldwide sales force and the Oracle Partner Network to businesses, governments, educational institutions, and resellers. Oracle reports three single operating segments—cloud and license, hardware, and services—and notes that cloud and license accounted for 86% of total revenues in fiscal 2025 and 84% in fiscal 2024, with the discussion focused on fiscal 2025 results versus fiscal 2024 and references to prior-year comparisons in the 2024 Form 10-K filed with the SEC.
sections.Item 5: Oracle Corporation’s common stock trades on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol “ORCL,” and the company reported 6,574 stockholders of record as of May 31, 2025. Oracle’s Board has an open-ended stock repurchase program with approximately $6.4 billion remaining authorization as of that date, and during the three months ended May 31, 2025 it repurchased 1.0 million shares at an average price of $146.22 per share through open market purchases or a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. The section also notes a five-year stock performance comparison against the S&P 500 Index and the Dow Jones U.S. Technology Total Return Index, using a $100 hypothetical investment with dividends reinvested.
sections.Item 9: Oracle Corporation (ORCL) reports no changes in accountants and no disagreements with accountants on accounting or financial disclosure for the 2025 Form 10-K. The Item 9 section states “None,” indicating no disputes, resignations, or related issues to disclose.
sections.Item 7A: Oracle Corporation says its market risk is driven mainly by equity price risk in non-marketable equity and convertible debt investments, which totaled $2.1 billion at May 31, 2025 versus $2.0 billion in 2024, with fair-value adjustments tied to observable transactions, impairment, and the performance of privately owned companies. The company also highlights significant foreign currency risk from its international operations, noting that U.S. Dollar strength affects translated revenues, expenses, assets, liabilities, earnings, and cash flows, and that transaction exposures arise primarily from intercompany activity in currencies including the Australian Dollar, Brazilian Real, British Pound, Euro, and Indian Rupee.
sections.Item 9C: Oracle Corporation (ORCL) states in Item 9C of its 2025 Form 10-K that the disclosure regarding foreign jurisdictions that prevent inspections is “Not applicable.” This section appears at the transition into Part III of the annual report and contains no additional substantive disclosure, metrics, or named regulators beyond the SEC filing context.
sections.Item 9B: Oracle Corporation (NYSE: ORCL) states in Item 9B that during the quarter ended May 31, 2025, none of its directors or officers, as defined in Rule 16a-1(f) of the Exchange Act, adopted or terminated any “Rule 10b5-1 trading arrangement” or “non-Rule 10b5-1 trading arrangement.” The disclosure references Rule 10b5-1 and Item 408 of Regulation S-K and indicates no changes to insider trading plans for the period.
sections.Item 9A: Oracle Corporation reports that, as of May 31, 2025, its disclosure controls and procedures were effective under Exchange Act Rules 13a-15(e) and 15d-15(e), based on an evaluation by management, the Disclosure Committee, and the Principal Executive and Financial Officer. Management also concluded that Oracle’s internal control over financial reporting was effective under the COSO 2013 Internal Control—Integrated Framework, with the assessment reviewed by the F&A Committee and audited by Ernst & Young LLP, and the company reported no changes in internal control over financial reporting.
sections.Item 13: Oracle Corporation (ORCL) states that the information required by Item 13, “Certain Relationships and Related Transactions, and Director Independence,” is incorporated by reference from its 2025 Proxy Statement. This section contains no substantive disclosure in the 10-K itself and directs readers to the proxy statement for details on related-party transactions and director independence.
sections.Item 11: Oracle Corporation (NYSE: ORCL) states that the information required by Item 11, Executive Compensation, is incorporated by reference from its 2025 Proxy Statement. This section contains no substantive compensation disclosure in the 10-K itself and directs readers to the proxy statement for details on executive pay and related governance matters.
sections.Item 12: Oracle Corporation (ORCL) states that the information required by Item 12, including security ownership of certain beneficial owners and management and related stockholder matters, is incorporated by reference from its 2025 Proxy Statement. This section contains no additional substantive disclosure in the 10-K excerpt and directs readers to the proxy statement for ownership and stockholder matter details.
sections.Item 10: Oracle Corporation’s Item 10 states that the required information about its executive officers is provided in Part I under “Information About Our Executive Officers,” pursuant to General Instruction G(3) of Form 10-K. The remaining directors, executive officers, and corporate governance disclosures are incorporated by reference from Oracle’s Proxy Statement to be filed with the SEC for the 2025 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, including the 2025 Proxy Statement.
sections.Item 14: Oracle Corporation (NYSE: ORCL) states that the information required by Item 14, Principal Accountant Fees and Services, is incorporated by reference from the information to be included in its 2025 Proxy Statement. This section of the 2025 Form 10-K contains no additional fee disclosures or auditor details beyond that cross-reference and the Part IV/Table of Contents placement.
sections.Item 15: Item 15 of Oracle Corporation’s 2025 Form 10-K lists the filed consolidated financial statements and notes, including balance sheets as of May 31, 2025 and 2024, statements of operations, comprehensive income, stockholders’ equity, and cash flows for the years ended May 31, 2025, 2024, and 2023, with the audit report by the independent registered public accounting firm (PCAOB ID: 42). It also states that all financial statement schedules are omitted as inapplicable or already covered in the financial statements or notes, and that the exhibits are provided in the Index of Exhibits after Item 16. The excerpt includes the auditor’s unqualified opinion that Oracle’s consolidated financial statements fairly present its financial position, results of operations, and cash flows in conformity with U.S. GAAP, and that internal control over financial reporting was also audited under PCAOB standards and the COSO 2013 framework.
sections.Item 16: Oracle Corporation’s Item 16 is an index of exhibits for its 2025 Form 10-K, listing charter documents, amended and restated bylaws, and a series of debt instruments and note forms incorporated by reference. The section references filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, including prior 8-K, 10-Q, S-3 ASR, and S-4 filings, and identifies key exhibits such as the Indenture with Citibank, N.A. and The Bank of New York Trust Company, N.A., plus notes due 2025, 2030, 2034, 2035, 2038, 2039, 2040, 2044, 2045, and 2055.

PEP_10K_2026-02-03_full.txt.document_context: PepsiCo Inc. (PEP) filed a 10-K annual report for the fiscal year ended December 27, 2025. sections.Preamble: PepsiCo, Inc. filed its Form 10-K annual report with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for the fiscal year ended December 27, 2025, identifying itself as a North Carolina corporation headquartered at 700 Anderson Hill Road, Purchase, New York, with Commission file number 1-1183 and telephone number (914) 253-2000. The filing lists PepsiCo common stock (PEP) and multiple Nasdaq-listed senior notes, including maturities from 2026 through 2055, and states that the company is a well-known seasoned issuer with no securities registered under Section 12(g).
sections.Item 1B: PepsiCo Inc. (PEP) states that it received no written comments from the staff of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) regarding its periodic or current reports that were issued 180 days or more before the end of fiscal 2025 and remain unresolved. This Item 1B disclosure indicates there were no outstanding SEC staff comment issues at year-end.
sections.Item 1A: PepsiCo Inc. (PEP) says human capital management is critical to long-term success, with Board oversight of culture, pay equity, health and safety, training, development, compensation and benefits, and approximately 306,000 employees worldwide as of December 27, 2025, including about 125,000 in the U.S. The company highlights generally good labor relations, numerous collective bargaining agreements, and investments in safety technologies such as fleet telematics, distracted driving tools, ergonomic solutions, and machine safety risk reduction to support an injury-free workplace. PepsiCo also emphasizes global talent development through programs like MyLearning and Schoox, noting employees completed over 2.8 million hours of training in 2025, and references SEC reporting obligations and availability of filings on the SEC website.
sections.Item 1: PepsiCo, Inc. describes its business as a global consumer products company operating through consolidated subsidiaries, with additional references to its segments, distribution network, ingredients and supplies, brands, intellectual property, seasonality, customers, competition, research and development, regulatory matters, and human capital. The section highlights governance and related-party relationships involving Board members and employees serving on boards of vendors, customers, and Pepsi Bottling Ventures LLC, while emphasizing commodity and supply-chain risks managed through fixed-price contracts, purchase orders, pricing agreements, and derivatives such as swaps and futures. It also notes exposure to inflation, geopolitical volatility, tariffs, weather, and climate-change regulation, including greenhouse-gas emissions rules and potential carbon pricing programs, which could affect commodity, transportation, labor costs, volumes, revenue, margins, and operating results.
sections.Item 1C: PepsiCo Inc. says it is regularly subject to cyberattacks and manages cybersecurity as part of its enterprise risk management framework, led by the Chief Strategy and Transformation Officer and Chief Information Security Officer. The company uses a National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) cybersecurity framework, internal and third-party penetration testing, tabletop exercises, phishing awareness training, and third-party risk controls for suppliers, software, and cloud providers, with security issues tracked and monitored.
sections.Item 7: PepsiCo Inc. describes itself as a leading global beverage and convenient food company, incorporated in Delaware in 1919 and reincorporated in North Carolina in 1986, with brands including Lay’s, Doritos, Cheetos, Gatorade, Pepsi-Cola, Mountain Dew, Quaker and SodaStream sold in more than 200 countries and territories. The company organizes operations into six reportable segments—PFNA, PBNA, IB Franchise, EMEA, LatAm Foods and Asia Pacific Foods—and notes that PFNA sells foods such as cereals, chips, dips and granola bars through independent distributors and retailers. PepsiCo also highlights the December 2024 acquisition of Strauss Group’s 50% stake in Sabra Dipping Company, making Sabra a wholly owned subsidiary, while cautioning investors about forward-looking statements and non-inclusive risk disclosures in Item 7 MD&A.
sections.Item 4: Item 4 states that PepsiCo Inc. has no mine safety disclosures applicable, indicating the company does not report mining operations or related MSHA matters in this 2026 Form 10-K. Flavell, and regional CEOs Athina Kanioura, Ram Krishnan, Silviu Popovici, and Eugene Willemsen.
sections.Item 2: PepsiCo Inc. says its principal executive office in Purchase, New York and facilities in Plano, Texas are its most significant corporate properties, and that its operations rely on manufacturing, processing, bottling, warehousing, distribution, storage, office, and research and development facilities that are either owned or leased. The section lists key segment properties across PFNA, PBNA, IB Franchise, EMEA, and LatAm Foods, including plants and R&D sites in Cedar Rapids, Valhalla, Arlington, Lehavim, Leicester, Kashira, Moscow, Celaya, Vallejo, Colonia, Cork, Singapore, Mexico City, and Hyderabad, with some land leased even where buildings are owned. PepsiCo also notes that most plants are held on a long-term basis and that its properties, together with those of contract manufacturers, co-packers, strategic alliances, and joint ventures, are in good operating condition and sufficient for current operations.
sections.Item 7A: PepsiCo Inc. (PEP) states in Item 7A that it provides quantitative and qualitative disclosures about market risk, covering the company’s exposure to changes in market conditions that could affect its financial results. This section of the 10-K is intended to describe PepsiCo’s market risk profile, including relevant financial instruments, sensitivities, and risk management considerations tied to interest rates, foreign exchange, commodities, and other market factors.
sections.Item 3: PepsiCo, Inc. reports multiple pending or recently resolved lawsuits alleging public nuisance and deceptive acts or practices tied to plastic pollution, brought by the New York Attorney General, the Mayor and City Council of Baltimore, County Counsel for Los Angeles County, and the Government of the United States Virgin Islands against PepsiCo and various subsidiaries including Frito-Lay, Frito-Lay North America, Pepsi Bottling Ventures LLC, and PepsiCo Caribbean, Inc. The New York court dismissed the complaint in full and the plaintiff appealed; Baltimore’s case was mostly dismissed with prejudice except for public nuisance and is stayed pending Maryland Supreme Court cases; Los Angeles dismissed Pepsi Bottling Ventures LLC; and the USVI case was removed to federal court with a remand motion pending. PepsiCo states the complaints do not specify damages sought and that it believes it has strong defenses.
sections.Item 5:
PepsiCo Inc. (PEP) states that its common stock trades on The Nasdaq Global Select Market and is also listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange, with approximately 87,254 shareholders of record as of January 23, 2026. sections.Item 12: PepsiCo Inc. reported common stock repurchases of 1.6 million shares in the fourth quarter of 2025 at an average price of $144.88 per share, all executed in open market transactions under its $10 billion repurchase program authorized by the Board on February 10, 2022, with $5.5 billion remaining available at quarter-end. The company also disclosed that on February 3, 2026 it launched a new $10 billion 2026 Share Repurchase Program, effective February 1, 2026 through February 28, 2030, replacing the prior program and allowing repurchases in open market, privately negotiated, accelerated stock repurchase, or other transactions. sections.Item 9B: During the 16 weeks ended December 27, 2025, PepsiCo Inc. reported that none of its directors or executive officers adopted, modified, or terminated any “Rule 10b5-1 trading arrangement” or “non-Rule 10b5-1 trading arrangement” as defined under Item 408 of Regulation S-K. sections.Item 10: PepsiCo Inc. sections.Preamble: PepsiCo, Inc. filed this Form 10-Q quarterly report with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for the 12 weeks ended March 25, 2023, listing its principal executive offices in Purchase, New York and identifying its North Carolina incorporation and IRS employer ID 13-1584302.
